#[test]\n    fn test_extract_query_terms() {\n        assert_eq!(extract_query_terms(&Some(\"hello world hello\".into())), vec![\"hello\", \"world\"]);\n        assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let resp = vec![\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 60.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 4500 })]) },\n        ];\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&resp);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_empty() {\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&[]);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(g.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n        });\n        let req = PreflightRequest { index_uid: \"test\".into(), terms: vec![\"search\".into()], filter: None };\n        let g = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n        });\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.degraded);\n        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n    ///\n    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    ///\n    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 1000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100_000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 10,\n            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify global aggregation\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n\n        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Node 0 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 1 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 2 fails\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"test\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        // Should aggregate from successful nodes only\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 80000);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"test\").unwrap().df, 8000);\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n            offset: 10,\n            limit: 20,\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n\n        // query must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n\n        // filter must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")));\n\n        // facets must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"facets\"), Some(&serde_json::json!([\"category\", \"tags\"])));\n\n        // custom body field must be preserved\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"custom\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"field\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body works with minimal request.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_minimal_request() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: None,\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: true, // Client requested scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit = 10\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(10)));\n\n        // offset must be 0\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body handles large offset/limit correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_pagination() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 100,\n            limit: 50,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false,\n            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit = 150 (fetch enough for coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(150)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when primary group has failed nodes.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_on_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: 2 nodes (healthy fallback targets)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await; // query_seq=0 → group 0\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up responses: all shards on group 1 nodes return valid data\n        let response_1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        });\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), response_1.clone());\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), response_1);\n\n        // All nodes in group 0 fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, shards should succeed via group 1\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should have succeeded via fallback (group 1)\n        assert!(!result.partial, \"Fallback should have succeeded\");\n        assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"No shards should have failed after fallback\");\n        assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have shard pages from fallback\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when both groups are down.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_all_groups_down() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // All nodes fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, should still fail when all groups are down\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.partial, \"Should be partial when all groups fail\");\n        assert!(!result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"Should have failed shards\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that partial policy does NOT use fallback.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_partial_policy_no_fallback() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Group 1 nodes are healthy but partial policy shouldn't use them\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"fallback-doc\"}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n        }));\n\n        // Group 0 nodes fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With partial policy, should NOT use fallback\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await.unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.partial, \"Should be partial\");\n        assert!(!result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"Should have failed shards\");\n        // Should NOT have any successful pages (fallback not used)\n        assert!(result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Partial policy should not use fallback\");\n    }\n\n    // ── plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor tests ─────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_with_version_floor_all_nodes_eligible() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 1, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..4 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // All nodes have version >= 1\n        let version_checker = |_index: &str, _node: &str| -> u64 { 10 };\n\n        let result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n            &topo,\n            0,\n            2,\n            64,\n            \"test_index\",\n            1,\n            &version_checker,\n            None,\n        ).await;\n\n        assert!(result.is_some(), \"Should succeed when all nodes eligible\");\n        let plan = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 64);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_with_version_floor_no_eligible_nodes() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 1, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..4 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // All nodes have version < floor\n        let version_checker = |_index: &str, _node: &str| -> u64 { 5 };\n\n        let result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n            &topo,\n            0,\n            2,\n            64,\n            \"test_index\",\n            10, // floor is 10\n            &version_checker,\n            None,\n        ).await;\n\n        assert!(result.is_none(), \"Should fail when no nodes eligible\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_with_version_floor_partial_eligibility() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 1, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-old\".into()), \"http://node-old:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-new\".into()), \"http://node-new:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        // Only node-new has version >= floor\n        let version_checker = |_index: &str, node: &str| -> u64 {\n            if node == \"node-new\" { 100 } else { 5 }\n        };\n\n        let result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n            &topo,\n            0,\n            2,\n            16,\n            \"test_index\",\n            10,\n            &version_checker,\n            None,\n        ).await;\n\n        assert!(result.is_some(), \"Should succeed with partial eligibility\");\n        let plan = result.unwrap();\n        // All shards should map to the eligible node\n        for node_id in plan.shard_to_node.values() {\n            assert_eq!(node_id.as_str(), \"node-new\", \"All shards should use eligible node\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── plan_search_scatter_for_group tests ─────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_for_specific_group() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                rg,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let result = plan_search_scatter_for_group(&topo, 0, 2, 64, 1, None).await;\n\n        assert!(result.is_some(), \"Should succeed for valid group\");\n        let plan = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 1, \"Should use specified group\");\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 64);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_for_invalid_group() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                rg,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let result = plan_search_scatter_for_group(&topo, 0, 2, 64, 99, None).await;\n\n        assert!(result.is_none(), \"Should fail for invalid group\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_for_group_rotation() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 1, 3);\n        for i in 0u32..4 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter_for_group(&topo, 0, 3, 64, 0, None).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n        // Verify intra-group replica rotation by checking shard_to_node\n        let node_0_usages = plan.shard_to_node.values()\n            .filter(|n| n.as_str() == \"node-0\")\n            .count();\n        let node_1_usages = plan.shard_to_node.values()\n            .filter(|n| n.as_str() == \"node-1\")\n            .count();\n        let node_2_usages = plan.shard_to_node.values()\n            .filter(|n| n.as_str() == \"node-2\")\n            .count();\n\n        // With RF=3 and query_seq=0, each shard should use replicas[0]\n        // The assignment should distribute shards across nodes\n        // Total should be 64 across all nodes\n        let node_3_usages = plan.shard_to_node.values()\n            .filter(|n| n.as_str() == \"node-3\")\n            .count();\n        assert!(node_0_usages + node_1_usages + node_2_usages + node_3_usages == 64);\n    }\n\n    // ── plan_search_scatter_adaptive tests ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_adaptive_basic() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                rg,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Create a selector with adaptive strategy\n        let selector = crate::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector::new(\n            crate::replica_selection::ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n                strategy: \"adaptive\".into(),\n                exploration_epsilon: 0.0, // Disable exploration for deterministic test\n                ..Default::default()\n            }\n        );\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter_adaptive(&topo, 0, 2, 64, &selector).await;\n\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 64);\n        assert!(plan.hedging_eligible, \"Should be eligible for hedging with multiple nodes in group\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_adaptive_empty_group() {\n        let topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2); // No nodes added\n\n        let selector = crate::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector::default();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter_adaptive(&topo, 0, 2, 64, &selector).await;\n\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n        assert!(plan.target_shards.is_empty(), \"Should have no targets for empty topology\");\n        assert!(!plan.hedging_eligible);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_adaptive_selector_returns_none() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 1, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..3 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Selector with adaptive strategy (will fall back to round-robin when no metrics)\n        let selector = crate::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector::new(\n            crate::replica_selection::ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n                strategy: \"adaptive\".into(),\n                exploration_epsilon: 0.0, // Disable exploration for deterministic test\n                ..Default::default()\n            }\n        );\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter_adaptive(&topo, 0, 2, 64, &selector).await;\n\n        // Should fall back to default behavior when no metrics exist\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 64);\n        assert!(plan.hedging_eligible, \"Should be eligible for hedging with multiple nodes\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that execute_scatter handles empty target_shards correctly.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_execute_scatter_empty_target_shards() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let mut plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 64, None).await;\n        plan.target_shards = Vec::new(); // Empty target shards\n\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should succeed with no pages and no failures\n        assert!(!result.partial);\n        assert!(result.shard_pages.is_empty());\n        assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test fallback with network error (not timeout).\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_fallback_with_network_error() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await;\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up responses for fallback\n        let response = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\"}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        });\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), response.clone());\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), response);\n\n        // Group 0 fails with network error\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::NetworkError(\"connection refused\".into()));\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::NetworkError(\"connection reset\".into()));\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should succeed via fallback\n        assert!(!result.partial);\n        assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that scatter_gather_search properly propagates deadline_exceeded.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_scatter_gather_deadline_exceeded() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n\n        let result = scatter_gather_search(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await;\n\n        // Should succeed but be degraded\n        assert!(result.is_ok());\n        let merged = result.unwrap();\n        assert!(merged.degraded);\n    }\n\n    // ── NodeClient trait methods tests  #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_get_task_status() {\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        let node = NodeId::new(\"test-node\".into());\n\n        let req = TaskStatusRequest {   };\n        c.write_responses.insert(node.clone(), custom_resp);\n\n        let req = WriteRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            documents: vec![],\n ─────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Test fallback when one group has empty replicas.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_fallback_with_empty_replicas_in_group() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: Only 1 node (not enough for RF=2, so assign_shard_in_group returns empty for some shards)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await; // query_seq=0 → group 0\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up responses: group 1 node returns valid data\n        let response_1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        });\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), response_1);\n\n        // All nodes in group 0 fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, some shards might succeed via group 1\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Result should be partial because group 1 has only 1 node (not enough for RF=2)\n        assert!(result.partial || !result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have partial success or some pages\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test fallback with partial success (some shards succeed via fallback, others fail).\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_fallback_partial_success() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes (all fail)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: 2 nodes (only one works)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await;\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up response: only node-g1-0 returns valid data\n        let response = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\"}],\n c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        // One group 1 node fails\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), NodeError::NetworkError(\"connection refused\".into()));\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_90f8732f2c884cfd81e6da64","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Task API endpoints: Miroir task namespace reconciliation.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n4\t//! - GET /tasks — List all Miroir tasks with Meilisearch-compatible filters (statuses, types, indexUids)\n5\t//! - GET /tasks/{id} — Get task status by mtask ID with per-node breakdown (polls nodes on each request)\n6\t//! - DELETE /tasks/{id} — Cancel a task (best-effort)\n7\t\n8\tuse axum::extract::{FromRef, Path, Query, State};\n9\tuse axum::http::StatusCode;\n10\tuse axum::{Json, Router};\n11\tuse miroir_core::scatter::{NodeClient, TaskStatusRequest};\n12\tuse miroir_core::task::{MiroirTask, TaskRegistry, TaskStatus, NodeTaskStatus};\n13\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n14\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n15\t\n16\tuse crate::client::HttpClient;\n17\tuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n18\t\n19\t/// Query parameters for GET /tasks (Meilisearch-compatible).\n20\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n21\tpub struct TasksQuery {\n22\t    /// Filter by status (comma-separated: \"succeeded,failed\")\n23\t    statuses: Option<String>,\n24\t    /// Filter by index UID (comma-separated: \"index1,index2\")\n25\t    indexUids: Option<String>,\n26\t    /// Filter by type (comma-separated: \"documentAdditionOrUpdate,documentDeletion\")\n27\t    types: Option<String>,\n28\t    /// Pagination: limit number of results\n29\t    limit: Option<usize>,\n30\t    /// Pagination: offset from start\n31\t    from: Option<usize>,\n32\t}\n33\t\n34\t/// Meilisearch-compatible task response.\n35\t#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n36\tpub struct TaskResponse {\n37\t    #[serde(rename = \"taskUid\")]\n38\t    pub task_uid: String,\n39\t    pub indexUid: Option<String>,\n40\t    pub status: String,\n41\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n42\t    pub task_type: Option<String>,\n43\t    pub details: Option<TaskDetails>,\n44\t    pub error: Option<TaskError>,\n45\t    pub duration: Option<String>,\n46\t    pub enqueuedAt: String,\n47\t    pub startedAt: Option<String>,\n48\t    pub finishedAt: Option<String>,\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\t/// Task details with per-node breakdown.\n52\t#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n53\tpub struct TaskDetails {\n54\t    /// Number of documents received (for document operations)\n55\t    pub received_documents: Option<usize>,\n56\t    /// Per-node task mapping\n57\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"HashMap::is_empty\")]\n58\t    pub nodes: HashMap<String, NodeTaskDetail>,\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\t/// Per-node task detail.\n62\t#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n63\tpub struct NodeTaskDetail {\n64\t    /// Local Meilisearch task UID on this node\n65\t    #[serde(rename = \"taskUid\")]\n66\t    pub task_uid: u64,\n67\t    /// Status of this node task\n68\t    pub status: String,\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t/// Task error information with per-node breakdown.\n72\t#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n73\tpub struct TaskError {\n74\t    pub code: String,\n75\t    pub message: String,\n76\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n77\t    pub error_type: String,\n78\t    /// Per-node error details\n79\t    pub details: HashMap<String, String>,\n80\t}\n81\t\n82\t/// Response for GET /tasks.\n83\t#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n84\tpub struct TasksListResponse {\n85\t    pub results: Vec<TaskResponse>,\n86\t    pub limit: usize,\n87\t    pub from: usize,\n88\t    pub total: usize,\n89\t}\n90\t\n91\t/// Build router for task endpoints.\n92\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n93\twhere\n94\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n95\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n96\t{\n97\t    Router::new()\n98\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::get(list_tasks::<S>))\n99\t        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::get(get_task::<S>))\n100\t        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_task::<S>))\n101\t}\n102\t\n103\t/// GET /tasks — List all Miroir tasks with optional filtering.\n104\tasync fn list_tasks<S>(\n105\t    Query(query): Query<TasksQuery>,\n106\t    State(state): State<S>,\n107\t) -> Result<Json<TasksListResponse>, StatusCode>\n108\twhere\n109\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n110\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n111\t{\n112\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n113\t\n114\t    // Parse status filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n115\t    let status_filter = query.statuses.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n116\t        s.split(',')\n117\t            .next()\n118\t            .and_then(|status_str| match status_str.trim() {\n119\t                \"succeeded\" | \"Succeeded\" => Some(TaskStatus::Succeeded),\n120\t                \"failed\" | \"Failed\" => Some(TaskStatus::Failed),\n121\t                \"processing\" | \"Processing\" => Some(TaskStatus::Processing),\n122\t                \"enqueued\" | \"Enqueued\" => Some(TaskStatus::Enqueued),\n123\t                \"canceled\" | \"Canceled\" => Some(TaskStatus::Canceled),\n124\t                _ => None,\n125\t            })\n126\t    });\n127\t\n128\t    // Parse indexUids filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n129\t    let index_uid_filter = query.indexUids.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n130\t        s.split(',')\n131\t            .next()\n132\t            .map(|uid| uid.trim().to_string())\n133\t    });\n134\t\n135\t    // Parse types filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n136\t    let task_type_filter = query.types.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n137\t        s.split(',')\n138\t            .next()\n139\t            .map(|ty| ty.trim().to_string())\n140\t    });\n141\t\n142\t    // Build filter with all parameters\n143\t    let filter = miroir_core::task::TaskFilter {\n144\t        status: status_filter,\n145\t        node_id: None,\n146\t        index_uid: index_uid_filter,\n147\t        task_type: task_type_filter,\n148\t        limit: query.limit,\n149\t        offset: query.from,\n150\t    };\n151\t\n152\t    // List tasks from registry\n153\t    let tasks = state\n154\t        .task_registry\n155\t        .list(filter)\n156\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n157\t\n158\t    // Get total count (without limit/offset)\n159\t    let total = state\n160\t        .task_registry\n161\t        .count();\n162\t\n163\t    // Convert to Meilisearch-compatible response\n164\t    let results = tasks.into_iter().map(task_to_response).collect();\n165\t\n166\t    let limit = query.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n167\t    let from = query.from.unwrap_or(0);\n168\t\n169\t    Ok(Json(TasksListResponse {\n170\t        results,\n171\t        limit,\n172\t        from,\n173\t        total,\n174\t    }))\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\t/// GET /tasks/{id} — Get a specific task by Miroir task ID.\n178\t///\n179\t/// Polls all mapped nodes for their current task status and aggregates the result.\n180\tasync fn get_task<S>(\n181\t    Path(id): Path<String>,\n182\t    State(state): State<S>,\n183\t) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\n184\twhere\n185\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n186\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n187\t{\n188\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n189\t\n190\t    // Validate task ID format\n191\t    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n192\t        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n193\t    }\n194\t\n195\t    let mut task = state\n196\t        .task_registry\n197\t        .get(&id)\n198\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n199\t        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n200\t\n201\t    // Poll nodes for current status if task is not terminal\n202\t    if !matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n203\t        let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n204\t        let client = HttpClient::new(\n205\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n206\t            state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n207\t        );\n208\t\n209\t        // Update node task statuses by polling each node\n210\t        let mut node_errors = HashMap::new();\n211\t        let mut any_processing = false;\n212\t        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n213\t        let mut any_failed = false;\n214\t\n215\t        for (node_id_str, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n216\t            let node_id = miroir_core::topology::NodeId::new(node_id_str.clone());\n217\t\n218\t            // Skip polling if node task is already terminal\n219\t            if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n220\t                if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n221\t                    any_failed = true;\n222\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n223\t                }\n224\t                continue;\n225\t            }\n226\t\n227\t            // Get node address from topology\n228\t            let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n229\t                Some(n) => n,\n230\t                None => {\n231\t                    node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), \"node not found in topology\".to_string());\n232\t                    any_failed = true;\n233\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n234\t                    continue;\n235\t                }\n236\t            };\n237\t\n238\t            // Poll this node for task status\n239\t            let req = TaskStatusRequest { task_uid: node_task.task_uid };\n240\t            match client.get_task_status(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n241\t                Ok(resp) => {\n242\t                    let new_status = resp.to_node_status();\n243\t                    // Update the node task status in the registry\n244\t                    let _ = state.task_registry.update_node_task(&id, node_id_str, new_status);\n245\t\n246\t                    // Track overall status\n247\t                    match new_status {\n248\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n249\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n250\t                            any_failed = true;\n251\t                            all_succeeded = false;\n252\t                            if let Some(error) = resp.error {\n253\t                                node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), error);\n254\t                            }\n255\t                        }\n256\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n257\t                            any_processing = true;\n258\t                            all_succeeded = false;\n259\t                        }\n260\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => {\n261\t                            all_succeeded = false;\n262\t                        }\n263\t                    }\n264\t                }\n265\t                Err(e) => {\n266\t                    tracing::warn!(node = %node_id_str, task = %id, error = ?e, \"failed to poll node for task\");\n267\t                    // Don't mark as failed on network error - may be transient\n268\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n269\t                }\n270\t            }\n271\t        }\n272\t\n273\t        // Update overall task status based on node task statuses\n274\t        let new_status = if any_failed {\n275\t            TaskStatus::Failed\n276\t        } else if all_succeeded {\n277\t            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n278\t        } else if any_processing {\n279\t            TaskStatus::Processing\n280\t        } else {\n281\t            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n282\t        };\n283\t\n284\t        // Record terminal task status in Prometheus (§10 task metrics)\n285\t        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n286\t            let status_str = match new_status {\n287\t                TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n288\t                TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n289\t                TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n290\t                _ => unreachable!(),\n291\t            };\n292\t            state.metrics.inc_tasks_total(status_str);\n293\t\n294\t            // Observe task processing age (time from creation to terminal state)\n295\t            let age_ms = {\n296\t                let now_ms = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n297\t                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n298\t                    .unwrap_or_default()\n299\t                    .as_millis() as u64;\n300\t                now_ms.saturating_sub(task.created_at)\n301\t            };\n302\t            state.metrics.observe_task_processing_age(age_ms as f64 / 1000.0);\n303\t        }\n304\t\n305\t        // Update the task status in the registry\n306\t        let _ = state.task_registry.update_status(&id, new_status);\n307\t\n308\t        // Update the task with node errors and new status\n309\t        task.status = new_status;\n310\t        task.node_errors = node_errors;\n311\t\n312\t        // Set timestamps\n313\t        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Processing) && task.started_at.is_none() {\n314\t            task.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n315\t                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n316\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n317\t                .as_millis() as u64);\n318\t        }\n319\t\n320\t        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) && task.finished_at.is_none() {\n321\t            task.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n322\t                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n323\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n324\t                .as_millis() as u64);\n325\t        }\n326\t    }\n327\t\n328\t    Ok(Json(task_to_response(task)))\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t/// DELETE /tasks/{id} — Cancel a task (best-effort).\n332\tasync fn delete_task<S>(\n333\t    Path(id): Path<String>,\n334\t    State(state): State<S>,\n335\t) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\n336\twhere\n337\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n338\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n339\t{\n340\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n341\t\n342\t    // Validate task ID format\n343\t    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n344\t        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n345\t    }\n346\t\n347\t    // Get the task first\n348\t    let task = state\n349\t        .task_registry\n350\t        .get(&id)\n351\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n352\t        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n353\t\n354\t    // Update status to canceled if not already terminal\n355\t    if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued | TaskStatus::Processing) {\n356\t        state\n357\t            .task_registry\n358\t            .update_status(&id, TaskStatus::Canceled)\n359\t            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n360\t    }\n361\t\n362\t    // Return the updated task\n363\t    let updated = state\n364\t        .task_registry\n365\t        .get(&id)\n366\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n367\t        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n368\t\n369\t    Ok(Json(task_to_response(updated)))\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\t/// Convert MiroirTask to Meilisearch-compatible TaskResponse.\n373\tfn task_to_response(task: MiroirTask) -> TaskResponse {\n374\t    let status_str = match task.status {\n375\t        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n376\t        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n377\t        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n378\t        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n379\t        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n380\t    };\n381\t\n382\t    let enqueued_at = format_millis_timestamp(task.created_at);\n383\t    let started_at = task.started_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n384\t    let finished_at = task.finished_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n385\t\n386\t    let error = if task.status == TaskStatus::Failed {\n387\t        Some(TaskError {\n388\t            code: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n389\t            message: task.error.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n390\t                if task.node_errors.is_empty() {\n391\t                    \"task failed\".to_string()\n392\t                } else {\n393\t                    format!(\"{} node(s) failed\", task.node_errors.len())\n394\t                }\n395\t            }),\n396\t            error_type: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n397\t            details: task.node_errors.clone(),\n398\t        })\n399\t    } else {\n400\t        None\n401\t    };\n402\t\n403\t    // Build per-node details\n404\t    let mut nodes = HashMap::new();\n405\t    for (node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n406\t        let node_status = match node_task.status {\n407\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n408\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n409\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n410\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n411\t        };\n412\t        nodes.insert(\n413\t            node_id.clone(),\n414\t            NodeTaskDetail {\n415\t                task_uid: node_task.task_uid,\n416\t                status: node_status.to_string(),\n417\t            },\n418\t        );\n419\t    }\n420\t\n421\t    let details = Some(TaskDetails {\n422\t        received_documents: None,\n423\t        nodes,\n424\t    });\n425\t\n426\t    TaskResponse {\n427\t        task_uid: task.miroir_id,\n428\t        indexUid: task.index_uid,\n429\t        status: status_str.to_string(),\n430\t        task_type: task.task_type,\n431\t        details,\n432\t        error,\n433\t        duration: None,\n434\t        enqueuedAt: enqueued_at,\n435\t        startedAt: started_at,\n436\t        finishedAt: finished_at,\n437\t    }\n438\t}\n439\t\n440\t/// Format milliseconds since epoch as ISO 8601 timestamp.\n441\tfn format_millis_timestamp(millis: u64) -> String {\n442\t    // Simple ISO 8601 format without chrono dependency\n443\t    let secs = millis / 1000;\n444\t    let millis_part = millis % 1000;\n445\t\n446\t    // Calculate date components (simplified, assumes Unix epoch)\n447\t    // This is a rough approximation - for production use chrono or time crate\n448\t    let days_since_epoch = secs / 86400;\n449\t    let seconds_in_day = secs % 86400;\n450\t\n451\t    let hours = seconds_in_day / 3600;\n452\t    let minutes = (seconds_in_day % 3600) / 60;\n453\t    let seconds = seconds_in_day % 60;\n454\t\n455\t    // Days from 1970-01-01 to 2000-01-01 is roughly 10957 days\n456\t    // This is a very rough approximation for formatting\n457\t    format!(\n458\t        \"{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}.{:03}Z\",\n459\t        1970 + days_since_epoch / 365,\n460\t        1 + (days_since_epoch % 365) / 30,\n461\t        1 + (days_since_epoch % 30),\n462\t        hours,\n463\t        minutes,\n464\t        seconds,\n465\t        millis_part\n466\t    )\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t#[cfg(test)]\n470\tmod tests {\n471\t    use super::*;\n472\t    use }\n565\t\n566\t    #[test]\n567\t    fn test_format_millis_timestamp() {\n568\t        let ts = format_millis_timestamp(1700000000000);\n569\t        assert!(ts.contains(\"T\"));\n570\t     let filter = TaskFilter::default();\n594\t        let tasks = registry.list(filter).unwrap();\n595\t        assert_eq!(tasks.len(), 1);\n596\t    }\n597\t}\n598\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c09ed84c-209f-4b4a-a5b5-db53b7e8964b","uuid":"53942807-c911-4e7c-8838-e1dde3307672","timestamp":"2026-05-24T03:31:04.555Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/tasks.rs","content":"//! Task API endpoints: Miroir task namespace reconciliation.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - GET /tasks — List all Miroir tasks with Meilisearch-compatible filters (statuses, types, indexUids)\n//! - GET /tasks/{id} — Get task status by mtask ID with per-node breakdown (polls nodes on each request)\n//! - DELETE /tasks/{id} — Cancel a task (best-effort)\n\nuse axum::extract::{FromRef, Path, Query, State};\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{NodeClient, TaskStatusRequest};\nuse miroir_core::task::{MiroirTask, TaskRegistry, TaskStatus, NodeTaskStatus};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Query parameters for GET /tasks (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct TasksQuery {\n    /// Filter by status (comma-separated: \"succeeded,failed\")\n    statuses: Option<String>,\n    /// Filter by index UID (comma-separated: \"index1,index2\")\n    indexUids: Option<String>,\n    /// Filter by type (comma-separated: \"documentAdditionOrUpdate,documentDeletion\")\n    types: Option<String>,\n    /// Pagination: limit number of results\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    /// Pagination: offset from start\n    from: Option<usize>,\n}\n\n/// Meilisearch-compatible task response.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    #[serde(rename = \"taskUid\")]\n    pub task_uid: String,\n    pub indexUid: Option<String>,\n    pub status: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub task_type: Option<String>,\n    pub details: Option<TaskDetails>,\n    pub error: Option<TaskError>,\n    pub duration: Option<String>,\n    pub enqueuedAt: String,\n    pub startedAt: Option<String>,\n    pub finishedAt: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task details with per-node breakdown.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskDetails {\n    /// Number of documents received (for document operations)\n    pub received_documents: Option<usize>,\n    /// Per-node task mapping\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"HashMap::is_empty\")]\n    pub nodes: HashMap<String, NodeTaskDetail>,\n}\n\n/// Per-node task detail.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct NodeTaskDetail {\n    /// Local Meilisearch task UID on this node\n    #[serde(rename = \"taskUid\")]\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n    /// Status of this node task\n    pub status: String,\n}\n\n/// Task error information with per-node breakdown.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskError {\n    pub code: String,\n    pub message: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub error_type: String,\n    /// Per-node error details\n    pub details: HashMap<String, String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for GET /tasks.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TasksListResponse {\n    pub results: Vec<TaskResponse>,\n    pub limit: usize,\n    pub from: usize,\n    pub total: usize,\n}\n\n/// Build router for task endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::get(list_tasks::<S>))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::get(get_task::<S>))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_task::<S>))\n}\n\n/// GET /tasks — List all Miroir tasks with optional filtering.\nasync fn list_tasks<S>(\n    Query(query): Query<TasksQuery>,\n    State(state): State<S>,\n) -> Result<Json<TasksListResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    // Parse status filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n    let status_filter = query.statuses.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n        s.split(',')\n            .next()\n            .and_then(|status_str| match status_str.trim() {\n                \"succeeded\" | \"Succeeded\" => Some(TaskStatus::Succeeded),\n                \"failed\" | \"Failed\" => Some(TaskStatus::Failed),\n                \"processing\" | \"Processing\" => Some(TaskStatus::Processing),\n                \"enqueued\" | \"Enqueued\" => Some(TaskStatus::Enqueued),\n                \"canceled\" | \"Canceled\" => Some(TaskStatus::Canceled),\n                _ => None,\n            })\n    });\n\n    // Parse indexUids filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n    let index_uid_filter = query.indexUids.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n        s.split(',')\n            .next()\n            .map(|uid| uid.trim().to_string())\n    });\n\n    // Parse types filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n    let task_type_filter = query.types.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n        s.split(',')\n            .next()\n            .map(|ty| ty.trim().to_string())\n    });\n\n    // Build filter with all parameters\n    let filter = miroir_core::task::TaskFilter {\n        status: status_filter,\n        node_id: None,\n        index_uid: index_uid_filter,\n        task_type: task_type_filter,\n        limit: query.limit,\n        offset: query.from,\n    };\n\n    // List tasks from registry\n    let tasks = state\n        .task_registry\n        .list(filter)\n        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n\n    // Get total count (without limit/offset)\n    let total = state\n        .task_registry\n        .count();\n\n    // Convert to Meilisearch-compatible response\n    let results = tasks.into_iter().map(task_to_response).collect();\n\n    let limit = query.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n    let from = query.from.unwrap_or(0);\n\n    Ok(Json(TasksListResponse {\n        results,\n        limit,\n        from,\n        total,\n    }))\n}\n\n/// GET /tasks/{id} — Get a specific task by Miroir task ID.\n///\n/// Polls all mapped nodes for their current task status and aggregates the result.\nasync fn get_task<S>(\n    Path(id): Path<String>,\n    State(state): State<S>,\n) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    // Validate task ID format\n    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n    }\n\n    let mut task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .get(&id)\n        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    // Poll nodes for current status if task is not terminal\n    if !matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n        let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n        let client = HttpClient::new(\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n            state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n        );\n\n        // Update node task statuses by polling each node\n        let mut node_errors = HashMap::new();\n        let mut any_processing = false;\n        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n        let mut any_failed = false;\n\n        for (node_id_str, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n            let node_id = miroir_core::topology::NodeId::new(node_id_str.clone());\n\n            // Skip polling if node task is already terminal\n            if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n                if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n                    any_failed = true;\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Get node address from topology\n            let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n                Some(n) => n,\n                None => {\n                    node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), \"node not found in topology\".to_string());\n                    any_failed = true;\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Poll this node for task status\n            let req = TaskStatusRequest { task_uid: node_task.task_uid };\n            match client.get_task_status(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    let new_status = resp.to_node_status();\n                    // Update the node task status in the registry\n                    let _ = state.task_registry.update_node_task(&id, node_id_str, new_status);\n\n                    // Track overall status\n                    match new_status {\n                        NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                        NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n                            any_failed = true;\n                            all_succeeded = false;\n                            if let Some(error) = resp.error {\n                                node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), error);\n                            }\n                        }\n                        NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                            any_processing = true;\n                            all_succeeded = false;\n                        }\n                        NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => {\n                            all_succeeded = false;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(node = %node_id_str, task = %id, error = ?e, \"failed to poll node for task\");\n                    // Don't mark as failed on network error - may be transient\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update overall task status based on node task statuses\n        let new_status = if any_failed {\n            TaskStatus::Failed\n        } else if all_succeeded {\n            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n        } else if any_processing {\n            TaskStatus::Processing\n        } else {\n            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n        };\n\n        // Record terminal task status in Prometheus (§10 task metrics)\n        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n            let status_str = match new_status {\n                TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n                TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n                TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n                _ => unreachable!(),\n            };\n            state.metrics.inc_tasks_total(status_str);\n\n            // Observe task processing age (time from creation to terminal state)\n            let age_ms = {\n                let now_ms = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .unwrap_or_default()\n                    .as_millis() as u64;\n                now_ms.saturating_sub(task.created_at)\n            };\n            state.metrics.observe_task_processing_age(age_ms as f64 / 1000.0);\n        }\n\n        // Update the task status in the registry\n        let _ = state.task_registry.update_status(&id, new_status);\n\n        // Update the task with node errors and new status\n        task.status = new_status;\n        task.node_errors = node_errors;\n\n        // Set timestamps\n        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Processing) && task.started_at.is_none() {\n            task.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64);\n        }\n\n        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) && task.finished_at.is_none() {\n            task.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(task_to_response(task)))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /tasks/{id} — Cancel a task (best-effort).\nasync fn delete_task<S>(\n    Path(id): Path<String>,\n    State(state): State<S>,\n) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    // Validate task ID format\n    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n    }\n\n    // Get the task first\n    let task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .get(&id)\n        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    // Update status to canceled if not already terminal\n    if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued | TaskStatus::Processing) {\n        state\n            .task_registry\n            .update_status(&id, TaskStatus::Canceled)\n            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n    }\n\n    // Return the updated task\n    let updated = state\n        .task_registry\n        .get(&id)\n        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    Ok(Json(task_to_response(updated)))\n}\n\n/// Convert MiroirTask to Meilisearch-compatible TaskResponse.\nfn task_to_response(task: MiroirTask) -> TaskResponse {\n    let status_str = match task.status {\n        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n    };\n\n    let enqueued_at = format_millis_timestamp(task.created_at);\n    let started_at = task.started_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n    let finished_at = task.finished_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n\n    let error = if task.status == TaskStatus::Failed {\n        Some(TaskError {\n            code: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n            message: task.error.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                if task.node_errors.is_empty() {\n                    \"task failed\".to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"{} node(s) failed\", task.node_errors.len())\n                }\n            }),\n            error_type: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n            details: task.node_errors.clone(),\n        })\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    // Build per-node details\n    let mut nodes = HashMap::new();\n    for (node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n        let node_status = match node_task.status {\n            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n        };\n        nodes.insert(\n            node_id.clone(),\n            NodeTaskDetail {\n                task_uid: node_task.task_uid,\n                status: node_status.to_string(),\n            },\n        );\n    }\n\n    let details = Some(TaskDetails {\n        received_documents: None,\n        nodes,\n    });\n\n    TaskResponse {\n        task_uid: task.miroir_id,\n        indexUid: task.index_uid,\n        status: status_str.to_string(),\n        task_type: task.task_type,\n        details,\n        error,\n        duration: None,\n        enqueuedAt: enqueued_at,\n        startedAt: started_at,\n        finishedAt: finished_at,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Format milliseconds since epoch as ISO 8601 timestamp.\nfn format_millis_timestamp(millis: u64) -> String {\n    // Simple ISO 8601 format without chrono dependency\n    let secs = millis / 1000;\n    let millis_part = millis % 1000;\n\n    // Calculate date components (simplified, assumes Unix epoch)\n    // This is a rough approximation - for production use chrono or time crate\n    let days_since_epoch = secs / 86400;\n    let seconds_in_day = secs % 86400;\n\n    let hours = seconds_in_day / 3600;\n    let minutes = (seconds_in_day % 3600) / 60;\n    let seconds = seconds_in_day % 60;\n\n    // Days from 1970-01-01 to 2000-01-01 is roughly 10957 days\n    // This is a very rough approximation for formatting\n    format!(\n        \"{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}.{:03}Z\",\n        1970 + days_since_epoch / 365,\n        1 + (days_since_epoch % 365) / 30,\n        1 + (days_since_epoch % 30),\n        hours,\n        minutes,\n        seconds,\n        millis_part\n    )\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::task::{NodeTask, NodeTaskStatus, TaskFilter};\n    use miroir_core::task_registry::TaskRegistryImpl;\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn        };\n\n        let response = task_to_response(task);\n        assert_eq!(response.task_uid, \"mtask-123\");\n        assert_eq!(response.status, \"succeeded\");\n       assert!(response.startedAt.is_some());\n        assert!(response.finishedAt.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(\n            response.details.unwrap().nodes.get(\"node-0\").unwrap().task_uid,\n            1\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_task_to_response_failed() {\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\n            \"node-0\".to_string(),\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_cf9c9c69d71246b596ebc0a8","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5 rules 0–5.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously:\n4\t//! the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them\n5\t//! deterministically in the order specified by §5.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! JWT signing-secret rotation (plan §9):\n8\t//! - Primary secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET`) signs new tokens; `kid` header identifies it.\n9\t//! - Optional previous secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS`) is present only during\n10\t//!   the rotation overlap window. Validation accepts either secret.\n11\t\n12\tuse axum::{\n13\t    extract::{FromRef, Request, State},\n14\t    http::{HeaderMap, Method},\n15\t    middleware::Next,\n16\t    response::{IntoResponse, subtle::ConstantTimeEq;\n28\t\n29\tuse crate::admin_session::{self, SealKey};\n30\t\n31\ttype HmacSha256 = Hmac<Sha256>;\n32\t\n33\t/// Extension carried in the request after successful cookie ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n48\t\n49\t/// Claims embedded in a search UI JWT session token.\n50\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n51\tpub struct JwtClaims {\n52\t    /// Subject — user identifier or \"anonymous\".\n53\t    pub sub: String,\n54\t    /// Index this token grants access to.\n55\t    pub idx: String,\n56\t    /// Granted scope (e.g. \"search\").\n57\t    pub scope: String,\n58\t    /// Issued-at timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n59\t    pub iat: u64,\n60\t    /// Expiration timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n61\t    pub exp: u64,\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t/// Key ID embedded in the JWT header to identify which secret signed it.\n65\tconst KID_PRIMARY: &str = \"primary\";\n66\tconst KID_PREVIOUS: &str = \"previous\";\n67\t\n68\t/// JWT header (always HS256).\n69\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n70\tstruct JwtHeader {\n71\t    alg: String,\n72\t    kid: String,\n73\t    typ: String,\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n77\t// Minimal HS256 JWT encode / decode (no external JWT crate needed)\n78\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n79\t\n80\t/// Encode and sign a JWT with the given secret.\n81\tfn jwt_encode(header: &JwtHeader, claims: &JwtClaims, secret: &[u8]) -> Result<String, String> {\n82\t    let header_json = serde_json::to_string(header).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n83\t    let claims_json = serde_json::to_string(claims).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n84\t\n85\t    let header_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(header_json.as_bytes());\n86\t    let payload_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(claims_json.as_bytes());\n87\t\n88\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64);\n89\t\n90\t    let mut mac =\n91\t        HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).map_err(|e| format!(\"HMAC init: {}\", e))?;\n92\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n93\t    let sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n94\t    let sig_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(sig);\n95\t\n96\t    Ok(format!(\"{}.{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64, sig_b64))\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t/// Decode and verify a JWT with the given secret. Returns (header, claims).\n100\tfn jwt_decode(\n101\t    token: &str,\n102\t    secret: &[u8],\n103\t) -> Result<(JwtHeader, JwtClaims), JwtValidationError> {\n104\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n105\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n106\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    let header_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n110\t        .decode(parts[0])\n111\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n112\t    let header: JwtHeader =\n113\t        serde_json::from_slice(&header_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n114\t\n115\t    if header.alg != \"HS256\" {\n116\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Verify signature\n120\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n121\t    let mut mac = HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).unwrap();\n122\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n123\t    let expected_sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n124\t\n125\t    let actual_sig = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n126\t        .decode(parts[2])\n127\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)?;\n128\t\n129\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n130\t    let sig_valid: bool = actual_sig.ct_eq(&expected_sig).into();\n131\t    if !sig_valid {\n132\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature);\n133\t    }\n134\t\n135\t    // Decode claims\n136\t    let claims_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n137\t        .decode(parts[1])\n138\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n139\t    let claims: JwtClaims =\n140\t        serde_json::from_slice(&claims_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n141\t\n142\t    // Check expiration with 30s leeway\n143\t    let now = epoch_seconds();\n144\t    if claims.exp + 30 < now {\n145\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired);\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    Ok((header, claims))\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n152\t// Auth state (shared via axum State)\n153\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n154\t\n155\t/// Configuration needed by the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n156\t#[derive(Clone)]\n157\tpub struct AuthState }\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n186\t// JWT signing / validation helpers\n187\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n188\t\n189\timpl AuthState {\n190\t    /// Create a new signed JWT session token for the given index.\n191\t    /// Always signs with the primary secret; `kid` header identifies it.\n192\t    pub fn sign_jwt(&self, sub: &str, idx: &str, scope: &str, ttl_s: u64) -> Option<String> {\n193\t        let secret = self.jwt_primary.as_ref()?;\n194\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n195\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n196\t            sub: sub.to_string(),\n197\t            idx: idx.to_string(),\n198\t            scope: scope.to_string(),\n199\t            iat: now,\n200\t            exp: now + ttl_s,\n201\t        };\n202\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n203\t            alg: secret.as_bytes()).ok()\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    /// Validate a JWT string against either the primary or previous secret.\n211\t    /// Returns the parsed claims if validation succeeds, or an error.\n212\t    pub fn validate_jwt(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, JwtValidationError> {\n213\t        // Try primary secret first\n214\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_primary {\n215\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n216\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n217\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n218\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch — try previous\n219\t            }\n220\t        }\n221\t\n222\t        // Try previous secret (rotation overlap window)\n223\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_previous {\n224\t            if secret.is_empty() {\n225\t                return Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty);\n226\t            }\n227\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n228\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n229\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n230\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch\n231\t            }\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)\n235\t    }\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t/// Errors returned by JWT validation.\n239\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n240\tpub enum JwtValidationError {\n241\t    /// Token structure is invalid or alg is not
