-----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // JWT signing and validation — primary secret\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn sign_and_validate_primary_jwt() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let claims = state.validate_jwt(&token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claims.sub, \"user1\");\n        assert_eq!(claims.idx, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(claims.scope, \"search\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn signed_jwt_authenticates_via_dispatch() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/indexes/products\",\n            Some(&token),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn expired_jwt_returns_jwt_invalid() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now - 3600,\n            exp: now - 100, // expired well beyond 30s leeway\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let token = jwt_encode(\n            &header,\n            &claims,\n            state.jwt_primary.as_ref().unwrap().as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::Expired));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn tampered_signature_returns_invalid_signature() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let mut token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        // Tamper with the signature\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n        token = format!(\"{}.{}.tampered_sig\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // JWT dual-secret rotation validation\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_old_token_validates_via_previous_secret() {\n        let primary = \"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\";\n        let previous = \"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\";\n\n        // Sign token with the previous secret\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now,\n            exp: now + 900,\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let old_token = jwt_encode(&header, &claims, previous.as_bytes()).unwrap();\n\n        // Simulate rotation — new primary, old primary as previous\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(previous.to_string()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        // Old token should still validate via previous secret\n        let validated = state.validate_jwt(&old_token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(validated.sub, \"user1\");\n\n        // And dispatch should authenticate it\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/indexes/products\",\n            Some(&old_token),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_new_token_validates_via_primary_secret() {\n        let state = test_state_with_dual_jwt();\n        let new_token = state.sign_jwt(\"user2\", \"orders\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let validated = state.validate_jwt(&new_token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(validated.sub, \"user2\");\n        assert_eq!(validated.idx, \"orders\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_wrong_secret_returns_invalid_signature() {\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(\"correct-secret-key-32bytes-long!!!\".to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(\"previous-secret-key-32bytes-long!!\".to_string()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        // Token signed with a completely different secret\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now,\n            exp: now + 900,\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let token = jwt_encode(\n            &header,\n            &claims,\n            \"wrong-secret-key-32bytes-long!!!!\".as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn leak_response_empty_previous_rejects_old_tokens() {\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(\"new-primary-secret-key-32bytes!!\".to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(String::new()), // empty = leak response\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(\"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJ0ZXN0In0.fake\");\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_after_step5_steady_state_previous_removed() {\n        let primary = \"final-primary-secret-key-32bytes\";\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        // Tokens signed with current primary work\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        assert!(state.validate_jwt(&token).is_ok());\n\n        // Old tokens signed with now-removed previous fail\n        let old_claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: epoch_seconds() - 100,\n            exp: epoch_seconds() + 800,\n        };\n        let old_header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let old_token = jwt_encode(\n            &old_header,\n            &old_claims,\n            \"old-previous-secret-now-removed\".as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n        assert!(state.validate_jwt(&old_token).is_err());\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // End-to-end rotation scenario\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn full_rotation_e2e() {\n        let secret_v1 = \"version-1-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n        let secret_v2 = \"version-2-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n\n        // Pre-rotation: only v1\n        let pre = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n        let token_v1 = pre.sign_jwt(\"alice\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        assert!(pre.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n\n        // During rotation: v2 primary, v1 previous\n        let during = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n        // Old token still validates\n        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n        // New tokens work too\n        let token_v2 = during.sign_jwt(\"bob\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n\n        // Post-rotation: only v2\n        let post = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n        // New token still works\n        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n        // Old token is rejected (signed with v1, no previous loaded)\n        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_err());\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match (admin_key only)\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_matches_admin_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/_miroir/some/endpoint\",\n            Some(\"admin-key-456\"),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_rejects_master_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/_miroir/some/endpoint\",\n            Some(\"master-key-123\"),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n     Some(\"wrong-key\"),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_rejects_missing_auth() {\n        let state = assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Rule 3 — master-key match (non-admin paths only)\n    // — missing auth → 401 miroir_invalid_auth\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn missing_auth_on_gated_endpoint_returns_invalid_auth() assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // X-Admin-Key short-circuit\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn x_admin_key_matches_admin_key() {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(\"X-Admin-Key\", \"admin-key-456\".parse().unwrap());\n        assert!(check_x_admin_key(&headers, b\"admin-key-456\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn x_admin_key_rejects_wrong_key() {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(\"X-Admin-Key\", \"wrong-key\".parse().unwrap());\n        assert!(!check_x_admin_key(&headers, b\"admin-key-456\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn x_admin_key_missing_header() {\n        let headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        assert!(!check_x_admin_key(&headers, b\"admin-key-456\"));\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Constant-time comparison\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_eq_matching() {\n        assert!(constant_time_compare(b\"hello\", b\"hello\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_eq_not_matching() {\n        assert!(!constant_time_compare(b\"hello\", b\"world\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_eq_different_lengths() {\n        assert!(!constant_time_compare(b\"short\", b\"much-longer-value\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_eq_empty() {\n        assert!(constant_time_compare(b\"\", b\"\"));\n    }\n\n    /// Timing-injection harness: verify no measurable delta between\n    /// \"all bytes wrong\" and \"one byte wrong\" comparisons at the same length.\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_no_timing_leak() {\n        use std::time::Instant;\n\n        let expected = b\"admin-key-456\";\n        let all_wrong = b\"xxxxxxxxxxxxx\";\n        let one_wrong = b\"admin-key-457\";\n\n        let iterations = 100_000u64;\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        for _ in 0..iterations {\n            let _ = constant_time_compare(all_wrong, expected);\n        }\n        let all_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        for _ in 0..iterations {\n            let _ = constant_time_compare(one_wrong, expected);\n        }\n        let one_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n\n        let ratio = all_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64() / one_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64();\n        assert!(\n            ratio > 0.5 && ratio < 2.0,\n            \"Timing ratio {} suggests non-constant-time comparison: all_wrong={:?}, one_wrong={:?}\",\n            ratio,\n            all_wrong_duration,\n            one_wrong_duration,\n        );\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Bearer extraction\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn extract_bearer_valid() {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(\"authorization\", \"Bearer my-token\".parse().unwrap());\n        assert_eq!(extract_bearer(&headers), Some(\"my-token\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn extract_bearer_missing_header() {\n        let headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        assert_eq!(extract_bearer(&headers), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn extract_bearer_wrong_scheme() {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(\"authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\".parse().unwrap());\n        assert_eq!(extract_bearer(&headers), None);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Admin path detection\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_detected() {\n        assert!(is_admin_path(\"/_miroir/metrics\"));\n        assert!(is_admin_path(\"/_miroir/admin/login\"));\n        assert!(is_admin_path(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/en\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn non_admin_path_not_detected() {\n        assert!(!is_admin_path(\"/indexes/products\"));\n        assert!(!is_admin_path(\"/search/products\"));\n        assert!(!is_admin_path(\"/health\"));\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Rate limiter stub\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn delete_raw(\n120\t        &self,\n121\t        address: &str,\n122\t        path: &str,\n123\t    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n124\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), path);\n125\t        let resp = self\n126\t            .client\n127\t            .delete(&url)\n128\t            .header(self.auth_header().0, &self.auth_header().1)\n129\t            .send()\n130\t            .await\n131\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n132\t        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n133\t        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n134\t        Ok((status, text))\n135\t    }\n136\t\n137\t    /// GET from a node — generic helper.\n138\t    pub async fn get_raw(\n139\t        &self,\n140\t        address: &str,\n141\t        path: &str,\n142\t    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n143\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), path);\n144\t        let resp = self\n145\t            .client\n146\t            .get(&url)\n147\t            .header(self.auth_header().0, &self.auth_header().1)\n148\t            .send()\n149\t            .await\n150\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n151\t        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n152\t        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n153\t        Ok((status, text))\n154\t    }\n155\t\n156\t    /// Get index statistics from Meilisearch.\n157\t    pub async fn get_index_stats(\n158\t        &self,\n159\t        address: &str,\n160\t        index_uid: &str,\n161\t    ) -> Result<Value, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n162\t        let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n163\t        let response = self\n164\t            .client\n165\t            .get(&url)\n166\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n167\t            .send()\n168\t            .await?;\n169\t\n170\t        if !response.status().is_success() {\n171\t            return Err(format!(\"Failed to get stats: {}\", response.status()).into());\n172\t        }\n173\t\n174\t        response.json().await.map_err(|e| e.into())\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    /// Get document frequency for a single term by searching.\n178\t    pub async fn get_term_df(\n179\t        &self,\n180\t        address: &str,\n181\t        index_uid: &str,\n182\t        term: &str,\n183\t        filter: &Option<Value>,\n184\t    ) -> Result<u64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n185\t        let url = format!(\n186\t            \"{}/indexes/{}/search\",\n187\t            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n188\t            index_uid\n189\t        );\n190\t\n191\t        let mut body = serde_json::json!({\n192\t            \"q\": term,\n193\t            \"limit\": 0,\n194\t        });\n195\t\n196\t        if let Some(f) = filter {\n197\t            body[\"filter\"] = f.clone();\n198\t        }\n199\t\n200\t        let response = self\n201\t            .client\n202\t            .post(&url)\n203\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n204\t            .json(&body)\n205\t            .send()\n206\t            .await?;\n207\t\n208\t        if !response.status().is_success() {\n209\t            return Err(format!(\"DF lookup failed: HTTP {}\", response.status()).into());\n210\t        }\n211\t\n212\t        let json: Value = response.json().await?;\n213\t        json.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n214\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n215\t            .ok_or_else(|| \"Failed to parse estimatedTotalHits\".into())\n216\t    }\n217\t\n218\t    /// Estimate average document length by sampling a few documents.\n219\t    pub async fn estimate_avg_doc_length(\n220\t        &self,\n221\t        address: &str,\n222\t        index_uid: &str,\n223\t    ) -> Result<f64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n224\t        let url = format!(\n225\t            \"{}/indexes/{}/documents\",\n226\t            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n227\t            index_uid\n228\t        );\n229\t\n230\t        let response = self\n231\t            .client\n232\t            .get(&url)\n233\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n234\t            .query(&[(\"limit\", \"10\")])\n235\t            .send()\n236\t            .await?;\n237\t\n238\t        if !response.status().is_success() {\n239\t            return Ok(500.0);\n240\t        }\n241\t\n242\t        let json: Value = response.json().await?;\n243\t        let results = json.get(\"results\").and_then(|v| v.as_array());\n244\t\n245\t        if let Some(docs) = results {\n246\t            if docs.is_empty() {\n247\t                return Ok(500.0);\n248\t            }\n249\t\n250\t            let mut total_length = 0u64;\n251\t            let mut field_count = 0u64;\n252\t\n253\t            for doc in docs {\n254\t                if let Some(obj) = doc.as_object() {\n255\t                    for (_key, value) in obj {\n256\t                        if let Some(s) = value.as_str() {\n257\t                            total_length += s.len() as u64;\n258\t                            field_count += 1;\n259\t                        }\n260\t                    }\n261\t                }\n262\t            }\n263\t\n264\t            if field_count > 0 {\n265\t                return Ok(total_length as f64 / field_count as f64);\n266\t            }\n267\t        }\n268\t\n269\t        Ok(500.0)\n270\t    }\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t/// Collect all healthy node addresses from config.\n274\tfn all_node_addresses(config: &Config) -> Vec<String> {\n275\t    config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect()\n276\t}\n277\t\n278\t/// Compute a fingerprint (SHA256) of settings as canonical JSON.\n279\t///\n280\t/// Canonical JSON sorts object keys to ensure consistent fingerprints\n281\t/// regardless of key ordering in the input.\n282\tfn fingerprint_settings(settings: &Value) -> String {\n283\t    // Canonicalize: sort object keys, no extra whitespace.\n284\t    let canonical = if settings.is_object() {\n285\t        if let Some(obj) = settings.as_object() {\n286\t            // Collect and sort keys.\n287\t            let mut sorted_entries: Vec<_> = obj.iter().collect();\n288\t            sorted_entries.sort_by_key(|&(k, _)| k);\n289\t            // Reconstruct as a Map with sorted keys.\n290\t            let mut sorted_map = serde_json::Map::new();\n291\t            for (key, value) in sorted_entries {\n292\t                sorted_map.insert(key.clone(), value.clone());\n293\t            }\n294\t            serde_json::to_string(&sorted_map).unwrap_or_default()\n295\t        } else {\n296\t            serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n297\t        }\n298\t    } else {\n299\t        serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n300\t    };\n301\t\n302\t    // SHA256 hash.\n303\t    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n304\t    hasher.update(canonical.as_bytes());\n305\t    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n306\t}\n307\t\n308\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n309\twhere\n310\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n311\t{\n312\t    Router::new()\n313\t        .route(\"/\", post(create_index_handler).get(list_indexes_handler))\n314\t        .route(\n315\t            \"/:index\",\n316\t            get(get_index_handler)\n317\t                .patch(update_index_handler)\n318\t                .delete(delete_index_handler),\n319\t        )\n320\t        .route(\"/:index/stats\", get(get_index_stats_handler))\n321\t        .route(\n322\t            \"/:index/settings\",\n323\t            get(get_settings_handler).patch(update_settings_handler),\n324\t        )\n325\t        .route(\n326\t            \"/:index/settings/*subpath\",\n327\t            get(get_settings_subpath_handler).patch(update_settings_subpath_handler),\n328\t        )\n329\t        .route(\"/:index/_preflight\", post(preflight_handler))\n330\t        .nest(\"/:index/documents\", documents::router::<S>())\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n334\t// POST /indexes — create index (broadcast + _miroir_shard)\n335\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n336\t\n337\tasync fn create_index_handler(\n338\t    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n339\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n340\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n341\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n342\t    let uid = body\n343\t        .get(\"uid\")\n344\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n345\t        .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(\n346\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n347\t            \"index creation requires a `uid` field\",\n348\t        ))?;\n349\t\n350\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n351\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n352\t    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n353\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n354\t\n355\t    // Phase 1: Create index on every node sequentially\n356\t    for address in &nodes {\n357\t        match client.post_raw(address, \"/indexes\", &body).await {\n358\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n359\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n360\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n361\t                }\n362\t                created_on.push(address.clone());\n363\t            }\n364\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n365\t                // Rollback: delete index on all previously created nodes\n366\t                rollback_delete_index(&client, uid, &created_on).await;\n367\t                let msg = format!(\n368\t                    \"index creation failed on node {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n369\t                    address, status, text\n370\t                );\n371\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n372\t            }\n373\t            Err(e) => {\n374\t                rollback_delete_index(&client, uid, &created_on).await;\n375\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n376\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n377\t                    format!(\"index creation failed on node {}: {}\", address, e),\n378\t                ));\n379\t            }\n380\t        }\n381\t    }\n382\t\n383\t    // Phase 2: Add `_miroir_shard` to filterableAttributes on every node.\n384\t    // Read current filterableAttributes from first node, merge `_miroir_shard`,\n385\t    // then broadcast the merged list to all nodes.\n386\t    let mut merged_attrs: Vec<Value> = vec![serde_json::json!(\"_miroir_shard\")];\n387\t\n388\t    if let Some(first_addr) = nodes.first() {\n389\t        match client.get_raw(first_addr, &format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", uid)).await {\n390\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n391\t                if let Ok(settings) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n392\t                    if let Some(existing) = settings.get(\"filterableAttributes\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n393\t                        for attr in existing {\n394\t                            let attr_str = attr.as_str().unwrap_or(\"\");\n395\t                            if attr_str != \"_miroir_shard\" && !attr_str.is_empty() {\n396\t                                merged_attrs.push(attr.clone());\n397\t                            }\n398\t                        }\n399\t                    }\n400\t                }\n401\t            }\n402\t            _ => {}\n403\t        }\n404\t    }\n405\t\n406\t    let filterable_patch = serde_json::json!({\n407\t        \"filterableAttributes\": merged_attrs\n408\t    });\n409\t\n410\t    let mut patch_ok: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n411\t    for address in &nodes {\n412\t        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", uid);\n413\t        match client.patch_raw(address, &path, &filterable_patch).await {\n414\t            Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n415\t                patch_ok.push(address.clone());\n416\t            }\n417\t            Ok((status, _text)) => {\n418\t                tracing::warn!(\n419\t                    node = %address,\n420\t                    status,\n421\t                    \"failed to set _miroir_shard filterable\"\n422\t                );\n423\t            }\n424\t            Err(e) => {\n425\t                tracing::warn!(\n426\t                    node = %address,\n427\t                    error = %e,\n428\t                    \"failed to set _miroir_shard filterable\"\n429\t                );\n430\t            }\n431\t        }\n432\t    }\n433\t\n434\t    if patch_ok.len() != nodes.len() {\n435\t        tracing::warn!(\n436\t            created = patch_ok.len(),\n437\t            total = nodes.len(),\n438\t            \"_miroir_shard filterableAttributes not set on all nodes\"\n439\t        );\n440\t    }\n441\t\n442\t    tracing::info!(\n443\t        index_uid = uid,\n444\t        nodes = nodes.len(),\n445\t        \"index created on all nodes\"\n446\t    );\n447\t\n448\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"uid\": uid, \"status\": \"created\"}))))\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\tasync fn rollback_delete_index(client: &MeilisearchClient, uid: &str, nodes: &[String]) {\n452\t    for address in nodes {\n453\t        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", uid);\n454\t        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n455\t            Ok(_) => tracing::info!(node = %address, \"rollback: deleted index\"),\n456\t            Err(e) => tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"rollback: failed to delete index\"),\n457\t        }\n458\t    }\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n462\t// GET /indexes — list indexes (proxy to first node)\n463\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n464\t\n465\tasync fn list_indexes_handler(\n466\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n467\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n468\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n469\t    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n470\t    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(&address.address, \"/indexes\").await.map_err(|e| {\n471\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"list indexes failed\");\n472\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n473\t    })?;\n474\t    if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n475\t        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"results\": []}));\n476\t        Ok(Json(json))\n477\t    } else {\n478\t        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n479\t    }\n480\t}\n481\t\n482\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n483\t// GET /indexes/{uid} — get single index (proxy)\n484\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n485\t\n486\tasync fn get_index_handler(\n487\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n488\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n489\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n490\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n491\t    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n492\t    let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index);\n493\t    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(&address.address, &path).await.map_err(|e| {\n494\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"get index failed\");\n495\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n496\t    })?;\n497\t    if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n498\t        Ok(Json(serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null)))\n499\t    } else {\n500\t        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n501\t    }\n502\t}\n503\t\n504\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n505\t// PATCH /indexes/{uid} — update index metadata (broadcast with rollback)\n506\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n507\t\n508\tasync fn update_index_handler(\n509\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n510\t    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n511\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n512\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n513\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n514\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n515\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n516\t    let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index);\n517\t\n518\t    // Snapshot current index state from all nodes before applying changes\n519\t    let mut snapshots: Vec<(String, Value)> = Vec::new();\n520\t    for address in &nodes {\n521\t        match client.get_raw(address, &path).await {\n522\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n523\t                let snapshot: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n524\t                snapshots.push((address.clone(), snapshot));\n525\t            }\n526\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n527\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(\n528\t                    status,\n529\t                    &text,\n530\t                    &format!(\"failed to snapshot index on {}: HTTP {}\", address, status),\n531\t                ));\n532\t            }\n533\t            Err(e) => {\n534\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n535\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n536\t                    format!(\"failed to snapshot index on {}: {}\", address, e),\n537\t                ));\n538\t            }\n539\t        }\n540\t    }\n541\t\n542\t    // Apply update sequentially to each node\n543\t    let mut applied: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n544\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n545\t\n546\t    for (address, _) in &snapshots {\n547\t        match client.patch_raw(address, &path, &body).await {\n548\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n549\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n550\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n551\t                }\n552\t                applied.push(address.clone());\n553\t            }\n554\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n555\t                rollback_index_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n556\t                let msg = format!(\n557\t                    \"index update failed on {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n558\t                    address, status, text\n559\t                );\n560\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n561\t            }\n562\t            Err(e) => {\n563\t                rollback_index_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n564\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n565\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n566\t                    format!(\"index update failed on {}: {}\", address, e),\n567\t                ));\n568\t            }\n569\t        }\n570\t    }\n571\t\n572\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"uid\": index, \"status\": \"updated\"}))))\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t/// Rollback index metadata updates by restoring pre-change snapshots.\n576\tasync fn rollback_index_update(\n577\t    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n578\t    path: &str,\n579\t    snapshots: &[(String, Value)],\n580\t    applied: &[String],\n581\t) {\n582\t    for address in applied {\n583\t        if let Some((_, snapshot)) = snapshots.iter().find(|(a, _)| a == address) {\n584\t            match client.patch_raw(address, path, snapshot).await {\n585\t                Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n586\t                    tracing::info!(node = %address, \"index update rollback succeeded\");\n587\t                }\n588\t                Ok((status, _text)) => {\n589\t                    tracing::error!(\n590\t                        node = %address,\n591\t                        status,\n592\t                        \"index update rollback failed\"\n593\t                    );\n594\t                }\n595\t                Err(e) => {\n596\t                    tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"index update rollback failed\");\n597\t                }\n598\t            }\n599\t        }\n600\t    }\n601\t}\n602\t\n603\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n604\t// DELETE /indexes/{uid} — broadcast delete\n605\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n606\t\n607\tasync fn delete_index_handler(\n608\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n609\t    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n610\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n611\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n612\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n613\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n614\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n615\t    let mut errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n616\t\n617\t    for address in &nodes {\n618\t        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index);\n619\t        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n620\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n621\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n622\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n623\t                }\n624\t            }\n625\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n626\t                errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n627\t            }\n628\t            Err(e) => {\n629\t                errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n630\t            }\n631\t        }\n632\t    }\n633\t\n634\t    if !errors.is_empty() && first_response.is_none() {\n635\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n636\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n637\t            format!(\"index deletion failed on all nodes: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")),\n638\t        ));\n639\t    }\n640\t\n641\t    if !errors.is_empty() {\n642\t        tracing::warn!(\n643\t            index_uid = %index,\n644\t            errors = errors.len(),\n645\t            \"index deletion partially failed\"\n646\t        );\n647\t    }\n648\t\n649\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"taskUid\": 0, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}))))\n650\t}\n651\t\n652\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n653\t// GET /indexes/{uid}/stats — fan out, aggregate\n654\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n655\t\n656\tasync fn get_index_stats_handler(\n657\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n658\t    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n659\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n660\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n661\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n662\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n663\t\n664\t    let mut total_docs: u64 = 0;\n665\t    let mut field_distribution: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n666\t    let mut success_count = 0;\n667\t\n668\t    for address in &nodes {\n669\t        match client.get_index_stats(address, &index).await {\n670\t            Ok(stats) => {\n671\t                success_count += 1;\n672\t                if let Some(n) = stats.get(\"numberOfDocuments\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n673\t                    total_docs += n;\n674\t                }\n675\t                if let Some(fd) = stats.get(\"fieldDistribution\").and_then(|v| v.as_object()) {\n676\t                    for (field, count) in fd {\n677\t                        if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n678\t                            *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n679\t                        }\n680\t                    }\n681\t                }\n682\t            }\n683\t            Err(e) => {\n684\t                tracing::warn!(node = %address, error = %e, \"stats fan-out failed\");\n685\t            }\n686\t        }\n687\t    }\n688\t\n689\t    if success_count == 0 {\n690\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n691\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n692\t            format!(\"stats unavailable for index `{}`: all nodes failed\", index),\n693\t        ));\n694\t    }\n695\t\n696\t    // Compute logical doc count: total_docs / (RG × RF)\n697\t    let rg = config.replica_groups as u64;\n698\t    let rf = config.replication_factor as u64;\n699\t    let divisor = rg * rf;\n700\t    let logical_docs = if divisor > 0 { total_docs / divisor } else { total_docs };\n701\t\n702\t    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n703\t        \"numberOfDocuments\": logical_docs,\n704\t        \"isIndexing\": false,\n705\t        \"fieldDistribution\": field_distribution,\n706\t    })))\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n710\t// GET /stats — global stats across all indexes\n711\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n712\t\n713\tpub async fn global_stats_handler(\n714\t    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n715\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n716\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n717\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n718\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n719\t\n720\t    // Get list of indexes from first node\n721\t    let first_address = nodes.first().ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(\n722\t        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n723\t        \"no nodes configured\",\n724\t    ))?;\n725\t\n726\t    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(first_address, \"/indexes\").await.map_err(|e| {\n727\t        MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, format!(\"failed to list indexes: {}\", e))\n728\t    })?;\n729\t\n730\t    if status < 200 || status >= 300 {\n731\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, \"failed to list indexes\"));\n732\t    }\n733\t\n734\t    let indexes: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n735\t    let index_list = indexes\n736\t        .get(\"results\")\n737\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_array())\n738\t        .cloned()\n739\t        .unwrap_or_default();\n740\t\n741\t    let mut total_docs: u64 = 0;\n742\t    let mut total_field_distribution: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n743\t\n744\t    for idx in &index_list {\n745\t        if let Some(uid) = idx.get(\"uid\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n746\t            for address in &nodes {\n747\t                match client.get_index_stats(address, uid).await {\n748\t                    Ok(stats) => {\n749\t                        if let Some(n) = stats.get(\"numberOfDocuments\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n750\t                            total_docs += n;\n751\t                        }\n752\t                        if let Some(fd) = stats.get(\"fieldDistribution\").and_then(|v| v.as_object()) {\n753\t                            for (field, count) in fd {\n754\t                                if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n755\t                                    *total_field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n756\t                                }\n757\t                            }\n758\t                        }\n759\t                    }\n760\t                    Err(_) => {}\n761\t                }\n762\t            }\n763\t        }\n764\t    }\n765\t\n766\t    let rg = config.replica_groups as u64;\n767\t    let rf = config.replication_factor as u64;\n768\t    let divisor = rg * rf;\n769\t    let logical_docs = if divisor > 0 { total_docs / divisor } else { total_docs };\n770\t\n771\t    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n772\t        \"databaseSize\": 0,\n773\t        \"lastUpdate\": \"\",\n774\t        \"indexes\": {},\n775\t        \"numberOfDocuments\": logical_docs,\n776\t        \"fieldDistribution\": total_field_distribution,\n777\t    })))\n778\t}\n779\t\n780\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n781\t// Settings: PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings — two-phase broadcast with verification (§13.5)\n782\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n783\t\n784\tasync fn update_settings_handler(\n785\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n786\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n787\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n788\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n789\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n790\t    two_phase_settings_broadcast(&state, &config, &index, \"/settings\", &body).await\n791\t}\n792\t\n793\tasync fn update_settings_subpath_handler(\n794\t    Path((index, subpath)): Path<(String, String)>,\n795\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n796\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n797\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n798\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n799\t    let path = format!(\"/settings/{}\", subpath);\n800\t    two_phase_settings_broadcast(&state, &config, &index, &path, &body).await\n801\t}\n802\t\n803\t/// Two-phase settings broadcast (§13.5):\n804\t/// Phase 1 (Propose): PATCH all nodes in parallel, collect task UIDs\n805\t/// Phase 2 (Verify): GET settings from all nodes, verify SHA256 fingerprints\n806\t/// Phase 3 (Commit): Increment settings_version, persist to task store\n807\t///\n808\t/// On hash mismatch, retry with exponential backoff up to max_repair_retries.\n809\t/// If unrepairable, raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert and freeze writes.\n810\tasync fn two_phase_settings_broadcast(\n811\t    state: &AppState,\n812\t    config: &Config,\n813\t    index: &str,\n814\t    settings_path: &str,\n815\t    body: &Value,\n816\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n817\t    // Use sequential strategy for rollback compatibility\n818\t    if config.settings_broadcast.strategy == \"sequential\" {\n819\t        return update_settings_broadcast_legacy(&config, index, settings_path, body).await;\n820\t    }\n821\t\n822\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n823\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(config);\n824\t    let full_path = format!(\"/indexes/{}{}\", index, settings_path);\n825\t\n826\t    // Check if a broadcast is already in flight\n827\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(index).await {\n828\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n829\t            MiroirCode::IndexAlreadyExists,\n830\t            format!(\"settings broadcast already in flight for index '{}'\", index),\n831\t        ));\n832\t    }\n833\t\n834\t    // Compute expected fingerprint of proposed settings\n835\t    let expected_fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(body);\n836\t\n837\t    // Set phase to Propose (1)\n838\t    state.metrics.set_settings_broadcast_phase(index, 1);\n839\t\n840\t    // Phase 1: Propose - PATCH all nodes in parallel\n841\t    let propose_fut = async {\n842\t        let mut node_task_uids = HashMap::new();\n843\t        let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n844\t        let mut errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n845\t\n846\t        for address in &nodes {\n847\t            match client.patch_raw(address, &full_path, body).await {\n848\t                Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n849\t                    if first_response.is_none() {\n850\t                        first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n851\t                    }\n852\t                    // Extract taskUid if present in response\n853\t                    if let Ok(resp) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n854\t                        if let Some(task_uid) = resp.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n855\t                            node_task_uids.insert(address.clone(), task_uid);\n856\t                        }\n857\t                    }\n858\t                }\n859\t                Ok((status, text)) => {\n860\t                    errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n861\t                }\n862\t                Err(e) => {\n863\t                    errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n864\t                }\n865\t            }\n866\t        }\n867\t\n868\t        (node_task_uids, first_response, errors)\n869\t    };\n870\t\n871\t    let (node_task_uids, first_response, propose_errors) = propose_fut.await;\n872\t\n873\t    if !propose_errors.is_empty() {\n874\t        state.metrics.clear_settings_broadcast_phase(index);\n875\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n876\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n877\t            format!(\"Phase 1 propose failed: {}\", propose_errors.join(\"; \")),\n878\t        ));\n879\t    }\n880\t\n881\t    // Start broadcast tracking\n882\t    state.settings_broadcast.start_propose(index.to_string(), body).await\n883\t        .map_err(convert_miroir_error)?;\n884\t\n885\t    // Set phase to Verify (2)\n886\t    state.metrics.set_settings_broadcast_phase(index, 2);\n887\t\n888\t    // Wait for all node tasks to complete (with timeout)\n889\t    let verify_timeout = Duration::from_secs(config.settings_broadcast.verify_timeout_s);\n890\t\n891\t    // Define verify logic as a closure that can be called multiple times\n892\t    // Uses parallel execution for performance (P5.5.b)\n893\t    let run_verify = || {\n894\t        let client = client.clone();\n895\t        let nodes = nodes.clone();\n896\t        let index = index.to_string();\n897\t        let settings_path = settings_path.to_string();\n898\t        async move {\n899\t            // Parallel verification: spawn GET requests to all nodes concurrently\n900\t            let verify_tasks: Vec<_> = nodes.iter().map(|address| {\n901\t                let client = client.clone();\n902\t                let address = address.clone();\n903\t                let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}{}\", index, settings_path);\n904\t                async move {\n905\t                    (address.clone(), client.get_raw(&address, &path).await)\n906\t                }\n907\t            }).collect();\n908\t\n909\t            let results: Vec<(String, Result<(u16, String), String>)> = join_all(verify_tasks).await;\n910\t\n911\t            let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\n912\t            let mut verify_errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n913\t\n914\t            for (address, result) in results {\n915\t                match result {\n916\t                    Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n917\t                        if let Ok(settings) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n918\t                            let hash = fingerprint_settings(&settings);\n919\t                            node_hashes.insert(address, hash);\n920\t                        }\n921\t                    }\n922\t                    Ok((status, text)) => {\n923\t                        verify_errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n924\t                    }\n925\t                    Err(e) => {\n926\t                        verify_errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n927\t                    }\n928\t                }\n929\t            }\n930\t\n931\t            (node_hashes, verify_errors)\n932\t        }\n933\t    };\n934\t\n935\t    let (mut node_hashes, verify_errors) = timeout(verify_timeout, run_verify())\n936\t        .await\n937\t        .map_err(|_| {\n938\t            MeilisearchError::new(\n939\t                MiroirCode::Timeout,\n940\t                \"Phase 2 verify timed out\",\n941\t            )\n942\t        })?;\n943\t\n944\t    if !verify_errors.is_empty() {\n945\t        state.settings_broadcast.abort(\n946\t            index,\n947\t            format!(\"Phase 2 verify failed: {}\", verify_errors.join(\"; \")),\n948\t        ).await.ok();\n949\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n950\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n951\t            format!(\"Phase 2 verify failed: {}\", verify_errors.join(\"; \")),\n952\t        ));\n953\t    }\n954\t\n955\t    // Enter verify phase and check hashes\n956\t    state.settings_broadcast.enter_verify(index, node_task_uids.clone()).await\n957\t        .map_err(convert_miroir_error)?;\n958\t\n959\t    // Retry loop with exponential backoff for hash mismatches\n960\t    let mut retry_count = 0u32;\n961\t    let max_retries = config.settings_broadcast.max_repair_retries;\n962\t\n963\t    loop {\n964\t        match state.settings_broadcast.verify_hashes(\n965\t            index,\n966\t            node_hashes.clone(),\n967\t            &expected_fingerprint,\n968\t        ).await {\n969\t            Ok(()) => break,\n970\t            Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::SettingsDivergence) => {\n971\t                state.metrics.inc_settings_hash_mismatch();\n972\t                retry_count += 1;\n973\t                if retry_count > max_retries {\n974\t                    state.settings_broadcast.abort(\n975\t                        index,\n976\t                        format!(\"max repair retries ({}) exceeded\", max_retries),\n977\t                    ).await.ok();\n978\t\n979\t                    // Freeze writes on this index if configured\n980\t                    if config.settings_broadcast.freeze_writes_on_unrepairable {\n981\t                        state.metrics.freeze_index_writes(index);\n982\t                        tracing::error!(\n983\t                            index = %index,\n984\t                            retries = max_retries,\n985\t                            \"settings divergence unrepairable - freezing writes on index\"\n986\t                        );\n987\t                    }\n988\t\n989\t                    // Raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert\n990\t                    state.metrics.raise_settings_divergence_alert(index);\n991\t\n992\t                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n993\t                        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n994\t                        format!(\"settings divergence detected after {} retries - writes frozen on index\", max_retries),\n995\t                    ));\n996\t                }\n997\t\n998\t                // Exponential backoff: 2^retry_count seconds, max 60s\n999\t                let backoff_ms = 1000 * (1u64 << (retry_count - 1).min(5));\n1000\t                tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(backoff_ms)).await;\n1001\t\n1002\t                // Identify mismatched nodes and reissue PATCH to them\n1003\t                let mismatched_nodes: Vec<_> = node_hashes.iter()\n1004\t                    .filter(|(_, hash)| hash.as_str() != expected_fingerprint)\n1005\t                    .map(|(node, _)| node.clone())\n1006\t                    .collect();\n1007\t\n1008\t                tracing::warn!(\n1009\t                    index = %index,\n1010\t                    retry = retry_count,\n1011\t                    mismatched_nodes = ?mismatched_nodes,\n1012\t                    \"settings hash mismatch detected - reissuing PATCH to mismatched nodes\"\n1013\t                );\n1014\t\n1015\t                // Reissue PATCH to mismatched nodes (repair)\n1016\t                let mut repair_errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n1017\t                for address in &mismatched_nodes {\n1018\t                    match client.patch_raw(address, &full_path, body).await {\n1019\t                        Ok((status, _text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n1020\t                            tracing::info!(\n1021\t                                node = %address,\n1022\t                                index = %index,\n1023\t                                retry = retry_count,\n1024\t                                \"successfully reissued settings PATCH\"\n1025\t                            );\n1026\t                        }\n1027\t                        Ok((status, text)) => {\n1028\t                            repair_errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n1029\t                        }\n1030\t                        Err(e) => {\n1031\t                            repair_errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n1032\t                        }\n1033\t                    }\n1034\t                }\n1035\t\n1036\t                if !repair_errors.is_empty() {\n1037\t                    state.settings_broadcast.abort(\n1038\t                        index,\n1039\t                        format!(\"repair reissue failed: {}\", repair_errors.join(\"; \")),\n1040\t                    ).await.ok();\n1041\t                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n1042\t                        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n1043\t                        format!(\"repair reissue failed: {}\", repair_errors.join(\"; \")),\n1044\t                    ));\n1045\t                }\n1046\t\n1047\t                // Re-run verify phase after repair\n1048\t                let (new_hashes, new_errors) = run_verify().await;\n1049\t                if !new_errors.is_empty() {\n1050\t                    state.settings_broadcast.abort(\n1051\t                        index,\n1052\t                        format!(\"re-verify failed: {}\", new_errors.join(\"; \")),\n1053\t                    ).await.ok();\n1054\t                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n1055\t                        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n1056\t                        format!(\"re-verify failed: {}\", new_errors.join(\"; \")),\n1057\t                    ));\n1058\t                }\n1059\t                node_hashes = new_hashes;\n1060\t            }\n1061\t            Err(e) => {\n1062\t                state.settings_broadcast.abort(index, e.to_string()).await.ok();\n1063\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n1064\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n1065\t                    e.to_string(),\n1066\t                ));\n1067\t            }\n1068\t        }\n1069\t    }\n1070\t\n1071\t    // Phase 3: Commit - increment settings version\n1072\t    let new_version = state.settings_broadcast.commit(index).await\n1073\t        .map_err(convert_miroir_error)?;\n1074\t\n1075\t    // Update settings version metric\n1076\t    state.metrics.set_settings_version(index, new_version);\n1077\t    state.metrics.clear_settings_broadcast_phase(index);\n1078\t\n1079\t    tracing::info!(\n1080\t        index = %index,\n1081\t        settings_version = new_version,\n1082\t        nodes = nodes.len(),\n1083\t        \"settings broadcast committed successfully\"\n1084\t    );\n1085\t\n1086\t    // Complete and remove from in-flight tracking\n1087\t    state.settings_broadcast.complete(index).await.ok();\n1088\t\n1089\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\n1090\t        \"taskUid\": 0,\n1091\t        \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n1092\t        \"settingsVersion\": new_version,\n1093\t    }))))\n1094\t}\n1095\t\n1096\t/// Legacy sequential settings broadcast: apply to nodes one-by-one, rollback on failure.\n1097\t///\n1098\t/// Kept for rollback compatibility when strategy: sequential.\n1099\tasync fn update_settings_broadcast_legacy(\n1100\t    config: &Config,\n1101\t    index: &str,\n1102\t    settings_path: &str,\n1103\t    body: &Value,\n1104\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n1105\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n1106\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(config);\n1107\t    let full_path = format!(\"/indexes/{}{}\", index, settings_path);\n1108\t\n1109\t    // Snapshot current settings from all nodes before applying changes\n1110\t    let mut snapshots: Vec<(String, Value)> = Vec::new();\n1111\t    for address in &nodes {\n1112\t        match client.get_raw(address, &full_path).await {\n1113\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n1114\t                let snapshot: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n1115\t                snapshots.push((address.clone(), snapshot));\n1116\t            }\n1117\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n1118\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(\n1119\t                    status,\n1120\t                    &text,\n1121\t                    &format!(\"failed to snapshot settings on {}: HTTP {}\", address, status),\n1122\t                ));\n1123\t            }\n1124\t            Err(e) => {\n1125\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n1126\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n1127\t                    format!(\"failed to snapshot settings on {}: {}\", address, e),\n1128\t                ));\n1129\t            }\n1130\t        }\n1131\t    }\n1132\t\n1133\t    // Apply settings sequentially\n1134\t    let mut applied: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n1135\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n1136\t\n1137\t    for (address, _snapshot) in &snapshots {\n1138\t        match client.patch_raw(address, &full_path, body).await {\n1139\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n1140\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n1141\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n1142\t                }\n1143\t                applied.push(address.clone());\n1144\t            }\n1145\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n1146\t                // Rollback all previously applied nodes\n1147\t                rollback_settings(&client, &full_path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n1148\t                let msg = format!(\n1149\t                    \"settings update failed on {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n1150\t                    address, status, text\n1151\t                );\n1152\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n1153\t            }\n1154\t            Err(e) => {\n1155\t                rollback_settings(&client, &full_path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n1156\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n1157\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n1158\t                    format!(\"settings update failed on {}: {}\", address, e),\n1159\t                ));\n1160\t            }\n1161\t        }\n1162\t    }\n1163\t\n1164\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"taskUid\": 0, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}))))\n1165\t}\n1166\t\n1167\t/// Rollback settings on previously-applied nodes using pre-change snapshots.\n1168\tasync fn rollback_settings(\n1169\t    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n1170\t    full_path: &str,\n1171\t    snapshots: &[(String, Value)],\n1172\t    applied: &[String],\n1173\t) {\n1174\t    for address in applied {\n1175\t        // Find the snapshot for this address\n1176\t        if let Some((_, snapshot)) = snapshots.iter().find(|(a, _)| a == address) {\n1177\t            match client.patch_raw(address, full_path, snapshot).await {\n1178\t                Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n1179\t                    tracing::info!(node = %address, \"settings rollback succeeded\");\n1180\t                }\n1181\t                Ok((status, _text)) => {\n1182\t                    tracing::error!(\n1183\t                        node = %address,\n1184\t                        status,\n1185\t                        \"settings rollback failed\"\n1186\t                    );\n1187\t                }\n1188\t                Err(e) => {\n1189\t                    tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"settings rollback failed\");\n1190\t                }\n1191\t            }\n1192\t        }\n1193\t    }\n1194\t}\n1195\t\n1196\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1197\t// GET /indexes/{uid}/settings — proxy to first node\n1198\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1199\t\n1200\tasync fn get_settings_handler(\n1201\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n1202\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n1203\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n1204\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n1205\t    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n1206\t    let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", index);\n1207\t    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(&address.address, &path).await.map_err(|e| {\n1208\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"get settings failed\");\n1209\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n1210\t    })?;\n1211\t    if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n1212\t        Ok(Json(serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null)))\n1213\t    } else {\n1214\t        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n1215\t    }\n1216\t}\n1217\t\n1218\tasync fn get_settings_subpath_handler(\n1219\t    Path((index, subpath)): Path<(String, String)>,\n1220\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n1221\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n1222\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n1223\t    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n1224\t    let path = Index lifecycle endpoints: create, delete, stats, settings broadcast.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.4 and P5.5 §13.5:\n//! - `POST /indexes` — create index on every node; auto-add `_miroir_shard` to\n//!   `filterableAttributes`; rollback on partial failure\n//! - `DELETE /indexes/{uid}` — broadcast delete to every node\n//! - `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats` — fan out, sum numberOfDocuments (logical count),\n//!   merge fieldDistribution\n//! - `PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings/*` — two-phase settings broadcast with verification\n//! - `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings/*` — proxy read from first node\n//! - `GET /stats` — global stats across all indexes\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path};\nuse axum::http::{HeaderMap, StatusCode};\nuse axum::routing::{get, post};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse futures_util::future::join_all;\nuse convert_miroir_error(e: MiroirError) -> MeilisearchError {\n    match e {\n        MiroirError::SettingsDivergence => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"settings divergence detected across nodes\",\n        ),\n        MiroirError::NotFound(msg) => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            format!(\"not found: {}\", msg),\n        ),\n        MiroirError::InvalidState(msg) => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            format!(\"invalid state: {}\", msg),\n        ),\n        _ => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            format!(\"settings broadcast error: {}\", e),\n        ),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Node client for communicating with Meilisearch.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct MeilisearchClient {\n    client: Client,\n    master_key: String,\n}\n\nimpl MeilisearchClient {\n    pub fn new(master_key: String) -> Self {\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_millis(10000))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client\");\n\n        Self { client, master_key }\n    }\n\n    fn auth_header(&self) -> (&str, String) {\n        (\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n    }\n\n    /// POST to a node — generic broadcast helper.\n    pub async fn post_raw(\n        &self,\n        address: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: &Value,\n    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), path);\n        let resp = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(self.auth_header().0, &self.auth_header().1)\n            .json(body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok((status, text))\n    }\n\n    /// PATCH to a node — generic broadcast helper.\n    pub async fn patch_raw(\n        &self,\n        address: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: &Value,\n    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), path);\n        let resp = self\n            .client\n            .patch(&url)\n            .header(self.auth_header().0, &self.auth_header().1)\n            .json(body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok((status, text))\n    }\n\n    /// DELETE on a node — generic helper.\n    pub async fn delete_raw(\n        &self,\n        address: &str,\n        path: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n
