&self.auth_header().1)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok((status, text))\n    }\n\n    /// GET from a node — generic helper.\n    pub async fn get_raw(\n        &self,\n        address: &str,\n        path: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), path);\n        let resp = self\n            .client\n            .get(&url)\n            .header(self.auth_header().0, &self.auth_header().1)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"request failed: {}\", e))?;\n        let status = resp.status().as_u16();\n        let text = resp.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"read body: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok((status, text))\n    }\n\n    /// Get index statistics from Meilisearch.\n    pub async fn get_index_stats(\n        &self,\n        address: &str,\n        index_uid: &str,\n    ) -> Result<Value, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n        let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .get(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await?;\n\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\n            return Err(format!(\"Failed to get stats: {}\", response.status()).into());\n        }\n\n        response.json().await.map_err(|e| e.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Get document frequency for a single term by searching.\n    pub async fn get_term_df(\n        &self,\n        address: &str,\n        index_uid: &str,\n        term: &str,\n        filter: &Option<Value>,\n    ) -> Result<u64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n        let url = format!(\n            \"{}/indexes/{}/search\",\n            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n            index_uid\n        );\n\n        let mut body = serde_json::json!({\n            \"q\": term,\n            \"limit\": 0,\n        });\n\n        if let Some(f) = filter {\n            body[\"filter\"] = f.clone();\n        }\n\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await?;\n\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\n            return Err(format!(\"DF lookup failed: HTTP {}\", response.status()).into());\n        }\n\n        let json: Value = response.json().await?;\n        json.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .ok_or_else(|| \"Failed to parse estimatedTotalHits\".into())\n    }\n\n    /// Estimate average document length by sampling a few documents.\n    pub async fn estimate_avg_doc_length(\n        &self,\n        address: &str,\n        index_uid: &str,\n    ) -> Result<f64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n        let url = format!(\n            \"{}/indexes/{}/documents\",\n            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n            index_uid\n        );\n\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .get(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .query(&[(\"limit\", \"10\")])\n            .send()\n            .await?;\n\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\n            return Ok(500.0);\n        }\n\n        let json: Value = response.json().await?;\n        let results = json.get(\"results\").and_then(|v| v.as_array());\n\n        if let Some(docs) = results {\n            if docs.is_empty() {\n                return Ok(500.0);\n            }\n\n            let mut total_length = 0u64;\n            let mut field_count = 0u64;\n\n            for doc in docs {\n                if let Some(obj) = doc.as_object() {\n                    for (_key, value) in obj {\n                        if let Some(s) = value.as_str() {\n                            total_length += s.len() as u64;\n                            field_count += 1;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            if field_count > 0 {\n                return Ok(total_length as f64 / field_count as f64);\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(500.0)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Collect all healthy node addresses from config.\nfn all_node_addresses(config: &Config) -> Vec<String> {\n    config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect()\n}\n\n/// Compute a fingerprint (SHA256) of settings as canonical JSON.\n///\n/// Canonical JSON sorts object keys to ensure consistent fingerprints\n/// regardless of key ordering in the input.\nfn fingerprint_settings(settings: &Value) -> String {\n    // Canonicalize: sort object keys, no extra whitespace.\n    let canonical = if settings.is_object() {\n        if let Some(obj) = settings.as_object() {\n            // Collect and sort keys.\n            let mut sorted_entries: Vec<_> = obj.iter().collect();\n            sorted_entries.sort_by_key(|&(k, _)| k);\n            // Reconstruct as a Map with sorted keys.\n            let mut sorted_map = serde_json::Map::new();\n            for (key, value) in sorted_entries {\n                sorted_map.insert(key.clone(), value.clone());\n            }\n            serde_json::to_string(&sorted_map).unwrap_or_default()\n        } else {\n            serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n        }\n    } else {\n        serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n    };\n\n    // SHA256 hash.\n    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n    hasher.update(canonical.as_bytes());\n    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", post(create_index_handler).get(list_indexes_handler))\n        .route(\n            \"/:index\",\n            get(get_index_handler)\n                .patch(update_index_handler)\n                .delete(delete_index_handler),\n        )\n        .route(\"/:index/stats\", get(get_index_stats_handler))\n        .route(\n            \"/:index/settings\",\n            get(get_settings_handler).patch(update_settings_handler),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/:index/settings/*subpath\",\n            get(get_settings_subpath_handler).patch(update_settings_subpath_handler),\n        )\n        .route(\"/:index/_preflight\", post(preflight_handler))\n        .nest(\"/:index/documents\", documents::router::<S>())\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// POST /indexes — create index (broadcast + _miroir_shard)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn create_index_handler(\n    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let uid = body\n        .get(\"uid\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"index creation requires a `uid` field\",\n        ))?;\n\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n\n    // Phase 1: Create index on every node sequentially\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.post_raw(address, \"/indexes\", &body).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if first_response.is_none() {\n                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                }\n                created_on.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                // Rollback: delete index on all previously created nodes\n                rollback_delete_index(&client, uid, &created_on).await;\n                let msg = format!(\n                    \"index creation failed on node {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n                    address, status, text\n                );\n                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                rollback_delete_index(&client, uid, &created_on).await;\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                    format!(\"index creation failed on node {}: {}\", address, e),\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Phase 2: Add `_miroir_shard` to filterableAttributes on every node.\n    // Read current filterableAttributes from first node, merge `_miroir_shard`,\n    // then broadcast the merged list to all nodes.\n    let mut merged_attrs: Vec<Value> = vec![serde_json::json!(\"_miroir_shard\")];\n\n    if let Some(first_addr) = nodes.first() {\n        match client.get_raw(first_addr, &format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", uid)).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if let Ok(settings) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n                    if let Some(existing) = settings.get(\"filterableAttributes\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n                        for attr in existing {\n                            let attr_str = attr.as_str().unwrap_or(\"\");\n                            if attr_str != \"_miroir_shard\" && !attr_str.is_empty() {\n                                merged_attrs.push(attr.clone());\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    let filterable_patch = serde_json::json!({\n        \"filterableAttributes\": merged_attrs\n    });\n\n    let mut patch_ok: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    for address in &nodes {\n        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", uid);\n        match client.patch_raw(address, &path, &filterable_patch).await {\n            Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n                patch_ok.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            Ok((status, _text)) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    node = %address,\n                    status,\n                    \"failed to set _miroir_shard filterable\"\n                );\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    node = %address,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to set _miroir_shard filterable\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if patch_ok.len() != nodes.len() {\n        tracing::warn!(\n            created = patch_ok.len(),\n            total = nodes.len(),\n            \"_miroir_shard filterableAttributes not set on all nodes\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    tracing::info!(\n        index_uid = uid,\n        nodes = nodes.len(),\n        \"index created on all nodes\"\n    );\n\n    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"uid\": uid, \"status\": \"created\"}))))\n}\n\nasync fn rollback_delete_index(client: &MeilisearchClient, uid: &str, nodes: &[String]) {\n    for address in nodes {\n        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", uid);\n        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n            Ok(_) => tracing::info!(node = %address, \"rollback: deleted index\"),\n            Err(e) => tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"rollback: failed to delete index\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// GET /indexes — list indexes (proxy to first node)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn list_indexes_handler(\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(&address.address, \"/indexes\").await.map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"list indexes failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n    if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"results\": []}));\n        Ok(Json(json))\n    } else {\n        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// GET /indexes/{uid} — get single index (proxy)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn get_index_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n    let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index);\n    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(&address.address, &path).await.map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"get index failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n    if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n        Ok(Json(serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null)))\n    } else {\n        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// PATCH /indexes/{uid} — update index metadata (broadcast with rollback)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn update_index_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n    let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index);\n\n    // Snapshot current index state from all nodes before applying changes\n    let mut snapshots: Vec<(String, Value)> = Vec::new();\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.get_raw(address, &path).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                let snapshot: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n                snapshots.push((address.clone(), snapshot));\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                return Err(forward_or_miroir(\n                    status,\n                    &text,\n                    &format!(\"failed to snapshot index on {}: HTTP {}\", address, status),\n                ));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                    format!(\"failed to snapshot index on {}: {}\", address, e),\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply update sequentially to each node\n    let mut applied: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n\n    for (address, _) in &snapshots {\n        match client.patch_raw(address, &path, &body).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if first_response.is_none() {\n                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                }\n                applied.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                rollback_index_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n                let msg = format!(\n                    \"index update failed on {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n                    address, status, text\n                );\n                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                rollback_index_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                    format!(\"index update failed on {}: {}\", address, e),\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"uid\": index, \"status\": \"updated\"}))))\n}\n\n/// Rollback index metadata updates by restoring pre-change snapshots.\nasync fn rollback_index_update(\n    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n    path: &str,\n    snapshots: &[(String, Value)],\n    applied: &[String],\n) {\n    for address in applied {\n        if let Some((_, snapshot)) = snapshots.iter().find(|(a, _)| a == address) {\n            match client.patch_raw(address, path, snapshot).await {\n                Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n                    tracing::info!(node = %address, \"index update rollback succeeded\");\n                }\n                Ok((status, _text)) => {\n                    tracing::error!(\n                        node = %address,\n                        status,\n                        \"index update rollback failed\"\n                    );\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"index update rollback failed\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DELETE /indexes/{uid} — broadcast delete\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn delete_index_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n    let mut errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n    for address in &nodes {\n        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index);\n        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if first_response.is_none() {\n                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                }\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !errors.is_empty() && first_response.is_none() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            format!(\"index deletion failed on all nodes: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    if !errors.is_empty() {\n        tracing::warn!(\n            index_uid = %index,\n            errors = errors.len(),\n            \"index deletion partially failed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"taskUid\": 0, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}))))\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// GET /indexes/{uid}/stats — fan out, aggregate\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn get_index_stats_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n\n    let mut total_docs: u64 = 0;\n    let mut field_distribution: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut success_count = 0;\n\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.get_index_stats(address, &index).await {\n            Ok(stats) => {\n                success_count += 1;\n                if let Some(n) = stats.get(\"numberOfDocuments\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                    total_docs += n;\n                }\n                if let Some(fd) = stats.get(\"fieldDistribution\").and_then(|v| v.as_object()) {\n                    for (field, count) in fd {\n                        if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n                            *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(node = %address, error = %e, \"stats fan-out failed\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if success_count == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            format!(\"stats unavailable for index `{}`: all nodes failed\", index),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Compute logical doc count: total_docs / (RG × RF)\n    let rg = config.replica_groups as u64;\n    let rf = config.replication_factor as u64;\n    let divisor = rg * rf;\n    let logical_docs = if divisor > 0 { total_docs / divisor } else { total_docs };\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"numberOfDocuments\": logical_docs,\n        \"isIndexing\": false,\n        \"fieldDistribution\": field_distribution,\n    })))\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// GET /stats — global stats across all indexes\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\npub async fn global_stats_handler(\n    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n\n    // Get list of indexes from first node\n    let first_address = nodes.first().ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n        \"no nodes configured\",\n    ))?;\n\n    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(first_address, \"/indexes\").await.map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, format!(\"failed to list indexes: {}\", e))\n    })?;\n\n    if status < 200 || status >= 300 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, \"failed to list indexes\"));\n    }\n\n    let indexes: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    let index_list = indexes\n        .get(\"results\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_array())\n        .cloned()\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n\n    let mut total_docs: u64 = 0;\n    let mut total_field_distribution: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for idx in &index_list {\n        if let Some(uid) = idx.get(\"uid\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            for address in &nodes {\n                match client.get_index_stats(address, uid).await {\n                    Ok(stats) => {\n                        if let Some(n) = stats.get(\"numberOfDocuments\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                            total_docs += n;\n                        }\n                        if let Some(fd) = stats.get(\"fieldDistribution\").and_then(|v| v.as_object()) {\n                            for (field, count) in fd {\n                                if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n                                    *total_field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    Err(_) => {}\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let rg = config.replica_groups as u64;\n    let rf = config.replication_factor as u64;\n    let divisor = rg * rf;\n    let logical_docs = if divisor > 0 { total_docs / divisor } else { total_docs };\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"databaseSize\": 0,\n        \"lastUpdate\": \"\",\n        \"indexes\": {},\n        \"numberOfDocuments\": logical_docs,\n        \"fieldDistribution\": total_field_distribution,\n    })))\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Settings: PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings — two-phase broadcast with verification (§13.5)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn update_settings_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    two_phase_settings_broadcast(&state, &config, &index, \"/settings\", &body).await\n}\n\nasync fn update_settings_subpath_handler(\n    Path((index, subpath)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let path = format!(\"/settings/{}\", subpath);\n    two_phase_settings_broadcast(&state, &config, &index, &path, &body).await\n}\n\n/// Two-phase settings broadcast (§13.5):\n/// Phase 1 (Propose): PATCH all nodes in parallel, collect task UIDs\n/// Phase 2 (Verify): GET settings from all nodes, verify SHA256 fingerprints\n/// Phase 3 (Commit): Increment settings_version, persist to task store\n///\n/// On hash mismatch, retry with exponential backoff up to max_repair_retries.\n/// If unrepairable, raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert and freeze writes.\nasync fn two_phase_settings_broadcast(\n    state: &AppState,\n    config: &Config,\n    index: &str,\n    settings_path: &str,\n    body: &Value,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Use sequential strategy for rollback compatibility\n    if config.settings_broadcast.strategy == \"sequential\" {\n        return update_settings_broadcast_legacy(&config, index, settings_path, body).await;\n    }\n\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(config);\n    let full_path = format!(\"/indexes/{}{}\", index, settings_path);\n\n    // Check if a broadcast is already in flight\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(index).await {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::IndexAlreadyExists,\n            format!(\"settings broadcast already in flight for index '{}'\", index),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Compute expected fingerprint of proposed settings\n    let expected_fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(body);\n\n    // Set phase to Propose (1)\n    state.metrics.set_settings_broadcast_phase(index, 1);\n\n    // Phase 1: Propose - PATCH all nodes in parallel\n    let propose_fut = async {\n        let mut node_task_uids = HashMap::new();\n        let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n        let mut errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n        for address in &nodes {\n            match client.patch_raw(address, &full_path, body).await {\n                Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                    if first_response.is_none() {\n                        first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                    }\n                    // Extract taskUid if present in response\n                    if let Ok(resp) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n                        if let Some(task_uid) = resp.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                            node_task_uids.insert(address.clone(), task_uid);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok((status, text)) => {\n                    errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        (node_task_uids, first_response, errors)\n    };\n\n    let (node_task_uids, first_response, propose_errors) = propose_fut.await;\n\n    if !propose_errors.is_empty() {\n        state.metrics.clear_settings_broadcast_phase(index);\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            format!(\"Phase 1 propose failed: {}\", propose_errors.join(\"; \")),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Start broadcast tracking\n    state.settings_broadcast.start_propose(index.to_string(), body).await\n        .map_err(convert_miroir_error)?;\n\n    // Set phase to Verify (2)\n    state.metrics.set_settings_broadcast_phase(index, 2);\n\n    // Wait for all node tasks to complete (with timeout)\n    let verify_timeout = Duration::from_secs(config.settings_broadcast.verify_timeout_s);\n\n    // Define verify logic as a closure that can be called multiple times\n    // Uses parallel execution for performance (P5.5.b)\n    let run_verify = || {\n        let client = client.clone();\n        let nodes = nodes.clone();\n        let index = index.to_string();\n        let settings_path = settings_path.to_string();\n        async move {\n            // Parallel verification: spawn GET requests to all nodes concurrently\n            let verify_tasks: Vec<_> = nodes.iter().map(|address| {\n                let client = client.clone();\n                let address = address.clone();\n                let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}{}\", index, settings_path);\n                async move {\n                    (address.clone(), client.get_raw(&address, &path).await)\n                }\n            }).collect();\n\n            let results: Vec<(String, Result<(u16, String), String>)> = join_all(verify_tasks).await;\n\n            let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\n            let mut verify_errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n            for (address, result) in results {\n                match result {\n                    Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                        if let Ok(settings) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n                            let hash = fingerprint_settings(&settings);\n                            node_hashes.insert(address, hash);\n                        }\n                    }\n                    Ok((status, text)) => {\n                        verify_errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        verify_errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (node_hashes, verify_errors)\n        }\n    };\n\n    let (mut node_hashes, verify_errors) = timeout(verify_timeout, run_verify())\n        .await\n        .map_err(|_| {\n            MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::Timeout,\n                \"Phase 2 verify timed out\",\n            )\n        })?;\n\n    if !verify_errors.is_empty() {\n        state.settings_broadcast.abort(\n            index,\n            format!(\"Phase 2 verify failed: {}\", verify_errors.join(\"; \")),\n        ).await.ok();\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            format!(\"Phase 2 verify failed: {}\", verify_errors.join(\"; \")),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Enter verify phase and check hashes\n    state.settings_broadcast.enter_verify(index, node_task_uids.clone()).await\n        .map_err(convert_miroir_error)?;\n\n    // Retry loop with exponential backoff for hash mismatches\n    let mut retry_count = 0u32;\n    let max_retries = config.settings_broadcast.max_repair_retries;\n\n    loop {\n        match state.settings_broadcast.verify_hashes(\n            index,\n            node_hashes.clone(),\n            &expected_fingerprint,\n        ).await {\n            Ok(()) => break,\n            Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::SettingsDivergence) => {\n                state.metrics.inc_settings_hash_mismatch();\n                retry_count += 1;\n                if retry_count > max_retries {\n                    state.settings_broadcast.abort(\n                        index,\n                        format!(\"max repair retries ({}) exceeded\", max_retries),\n                    ).await.ok();\n\n                    // Freeze writes on this index if configured\n                    if config.settings_broadcast.freeze_writes_on_unrepairable {\n                        state.metrics.freeze_index_writes(index);\n                        tracing::error!(\n                            index = %index,\n                            retries = max_retries,\n                            \"settings divergence unrepairable - freezing writes on index\"\n                        );\n                    }\n\n                    // Raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert\n                    state.metrics.raise_settings_divergence_alert(index);\n\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                        format!(\"settings divergence detected after {} retries - writes frozen on index\", max_retries),\n                    ));\n                }\n\n                // Exponential backoff: 2^retry_count seconds, max 60s\n                let backoff_ms = 1000 * (1u64 << (retry_count - 1).min(5));\n                tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(backoff_ms)).await;\n\n                // Identify mismatched nodes and reissue PATCH to them\n                let mismatched_nodes: Vec<_> = node_hashes.iter()\n                    .filter(|(_, hash)| hash.as_str() != expected_fingerprint)\n                    .map(|(node, _)| node.clone())\n                    .collect();\n\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    index = %index,\n                    retry = retry_count,\n                    mismatched_nodes = ?mismatched_nodes,\n                    \"settings hash mismatch detected - reissuing PATCH to mismatched nodes\"\n                );\n\n                // Reissue PATCH to mismatched nodes (repair)\n                let mut repair_errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n                for address in &mismatched_nodes {\n                    match client.patch_raw(address, &full_path, body).await {\n                        Ok((status, _text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                            tracing::info!(\n                                node = %address,\n                                index = %index,\n                                retry = retry_count,\n                                \"successfully reissued settings PATCH\"\n                            );\n                        }\n                        Ok((status, text)) => {\n                            repair_errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n                        }\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            repair_errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if !repair_errors.is_empty() {\n                    state.settings_broadcast.abort(\n                        index,\n                        format!(\"repair reissue failed: {}\", repair_errors.join(\"; \")),\n                    ).await.ok();\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                        format!(\"repair reissue failed: {}\", repair_errors.join(\"; \")),\n                    ));\n                }\n\n                // Re-run verify phase after repair\n                let (new_hashes, new_errors) = run_verify().await;\n                if !new_errors.is_empty() {\n                    state.settings_broadcast.abort(\n                        index,\n                        format!(\"re-verify failed: {}\", new_errors.join(\"; \")),\n                    ).await.ok();\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                        format!(\"re-verify failed: {}\", new_errors.join(\"; \")),\n                    ));\n                }\n                node_hashes = new_hashes;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                state.settings_broadcast.abort(index, e.to_string()).await.ok();\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                    e.to_string(),\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Phase 3: Commit - increment settings version\n    let new_version = state.settings_broadcast.commit(index).await\n        .map_err(convert_miroir_error)?;\n\n    // Update settings version metric\n    state.metrics.set_settings_version(index, new_version);\n    state.metrics.clear_settings_broadcast_phase(index);\n\n    tracing::info!(\n        index = %index,\n        settings_version = new_version,\n        nodes = nodes.len(),\n        \"settings broadcast committed successfully\"\n    );\n\n    // Complete and remove from in-flight tracking\n    state.settings_broadcast.complete(index).await.ok();\n\n    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\n        \"taskUid\": 0,\n        \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n        \"settingsVersion\": new_version,\n    }))))\n}\n\n/// Legacy sequential settings broadcast: apply to nodes one-by-one, rollback on failure.\n///\n/// Kept for rollback compatibility when strategy: sequential.\nasync fn update_settings_broadcast_legacy(\n    config: &Config,\n    index: &str,\n    settings_path: &str,\n    body: &Value,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(config);\n    let full_path = format!(\"/indexes/{}{}\", index, settings_path);\n\n    // Snapshot current settings from all nodes before applying changes\n    let mut snapshots: Vec<(String, Value)> = Vec::new();\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.get_raw(address, &full_path).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                let snapshot: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n                snapshots.push((address.clone(), snapshot));\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                return Err(forward_or_miroir(\n                    status,\n                    &text,\n                    &format!(\"failed to snapshot settings on {}: HTTP {}\", address, status),\n                ));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                    format!(\"failed to snapshot settings on {}: {}\", address, e),\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply settings sequentially\n    let mut applied: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n\n    for (address, _snapshot) in &snapshots {\n        match client.patch_raw(address, &full_path, body).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if first_response.is_none() {\n                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                }\n                applied.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                // Rollback all previously applied nodes\n                rollback_settings(&client, &full_path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n                let msg = format!(\n                    \"settings update failed on {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n                    address, status, text\n                );\n                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                rollback_settings(&client, &full_path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                    format!(\"settings update failed on {}: {}\", address, e),\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"taskUid\": 0, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}))))\n}\n\n/// Rollback settings on previously-applied nodes using pre-change snapshots.\nasync fn rollback_settings(\n    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n    full_path: &str,\n    snapshots: &[(String, Value)],\n    applied: &[String],\n) {\n    for address in applied {\n        // Find the snapshot for this address\n        if let Some((_, snapshot)) = snapshots.iter().find(|(a, _)| a == address) {\n            match client.patch_raw(address, full_path, snapshot).await {\n                Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n                    tracing::info!(node = %address, \"settings rollback succeeded\");\n                }\n                Ok((status, _text)) => {\n                    tracing::error!(\n                        node = %address,\n                        status,\n                        \"settings rollback failed\"\n                    );\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"settings rollback failed\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// GET /indexes/{uid}/settings — proxy to first node\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn get_settings_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, StatusCode> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let address = config.nodes.first().ok_or(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;\n    let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", index);\n    let (status, text) = client.get_raw(&address.address, &path).await.map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"get settings failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n    if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n        Ok(Json(serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null)))\n    } else {\n        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n    }\n}\n\nasync fn get_settings_subpath_handler(\n    Path((index, subpath)): Path<(String, String)>,\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// POST /indexes/{uid}/_preflight — DFS preflight\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn preflight_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n    Extension(_topology): Extension<Arc<Topology>>,\n    Json(body): Json<PreflightRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<PreflightResponse>, StatusCode> {\n    let node = config\n        .nodes\n        .first()\n        .ok_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n\n    let total_docs = client\n        .get_index_stats(&node.address, &index)\n        .await\n        .and_then(|v| {\n            v.get(\"numberOfDocuments\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                .ok_or_else(|| \"Failed to parse numberOfDocuments\".into())\n        })\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to get index stats\");\n            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n        })?;\n\n    let avg_doc_length = client\n        .estimate_avg_doc_length(&node.address, &index)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or(500.0);\n\n    let mut term_stats = HashMap::new();\n    for term ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Try to forward a Meilisearch error from a node response; fall back to a Miroir error.\nfn forward_or_miroir(_status: u16, body: &str, fallback_msg: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n    if let Some(meili_err) = MeilisearchError::forwarded(body) {\n        meili_err\n    } else {\n        MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, fallback_msg)\n    }\n}\n","numLines":1302,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1302}}}

tokio::sync::RwLock;\n31\tuse tracing::{info, error, warn};\n32\tuse reqwest::Client;\n33\t\n34\tuse crate::{\n35\t    admin_session::{seal_session, COOKIE_NAME, SealKey},\n36\t    client::HttpClient,\n37\t    scoped_key_rotation::{self, ScopedKeyRotationState, RotateScopedKeyRequest, RotateScopedKeyResponse},\n38\t};\n39\t\n40\t/// Hash a PII value (IP address) for safe log correlation.\n41\tfn hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n42\t    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n43\t    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n44\t    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n45\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n46\t}\n47\t\n48\t/// Request body for POST /_miroir/admin/login.\n49\t#[derive(Deserialize)]\n50\tpub struct AdminLoginRequest {\n51\t    pub admin_key: String,\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\timpl std::fmt::Debug for AdminLoginRequest {\n55\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n56\t        f.debug_struct(\"AdminLoginRequest\")\n57\t            .field(\"admin_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n58\t            .finish()\n59\t    }\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t/// Response body for POST /_miroir/admin/login.\n63\t#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n64\tpub struct AdminLoginResponse {\n65\t    pub success: bool,\n66\t    pub message: Option<String>,\n67\t}\n68\t\n69\t/// Version state with cache for fetching Meilisearch version.\n70\t#[derive(Clone)]\n71\tpub struct VersionState {\n72\t    pub node_master_key: String,\n73\t    pub node_addresses: Vec<String>,\n74\t    pub version_cache: Arc<RwLock<Option<String>>>,\n75\t    pub last_cache_update: Arc<RwLock<Option<std::time::Instant>>>,\n76\t    pub cache_ttl_secs: u64,\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\timpl VersionState {\n80\t    pub fn new(node_master_key: String, node_addresses: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n81\t        Self {\n82\t            node_master_key,\n83\t            node_addresses,\n84\t            version_cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n85\t            last_cache_update: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n86\t            cache_ttl_secs: 60,\n87\t        }\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    /// Fetch version from a healthy node, using cache if within TTL.\n91\t    pub async fn get_version(&self) -> Result<String, StatusCode> {\n92\t        // Check cache first\n93\t        {\n94\t            let cache = self.version_cache.read().await;\n95\t            let last_update = self.last_cache_update.read().await;\n96\t            if let (Some(ref cached), Some(last)) = (cache.as_ref(), last_update.as_ref()) {\n97\t                if last.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_ttl_secs {\n98\t                    return Ok((**cached).clone());\n99\t                }\n100\t            }\n101\t        }\n102\t\n103\t        // Cache miss or expired - fetch from a node\n104\t        let client = Client::builder()\n105\t            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))\n106\t            .build()\n107\t            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n108\t\n109\t        for address in &self.node_addresses {\n110\t            let url = format!(\"{}/version\", address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n111\t            let response = client\n112\t                .get(&url)\n113\t                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n114\t                .send()\n115\t                .await;\n116\t\n117\t            if let Ok(resp) = response {\n118\t                if resp.status().is_success() {\n119\t                    if let Ok(body) = resp.text().await {\n120\t                        // Update cache\n121\t                        *self.version_cache.write().await = Some(body.clone());\n122\t                        *self.last_cache_update.write().await = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n123\t                        return Ok(body);\n124\t                    }\n125\t                }\n126\t            }\n127\t        }\n128\t\n129\t        Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n130\t    }\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n134\t// Local Rate Limiter (for single-pod deployments)\n135\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n136\t\n137\t/// In-memory rate limiter for admin login (local backend only).\n138\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n139\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n140\tpub struct LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n141\t    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalAdminRateLimiterInner>>,\n142\t}\n143\t\n144\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n145\tstruct LocalAdminRateLimiterInner {\n146\t    /// Map of IP -> (request_timestamps_ms, failed_count, backoff_until_ms)\n147\t    state: HashMap<String, LocalRateLimitState>,\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\t#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\n151\tstruct LocalRateLimitState {\n152\t    /// Timestamps of recent requests (for sliding window)\n153\t    request_timestamps_ms: Vec<i64>,\n154\t    /// Consecutive failed login attempts\n155\t    failed_count: u32,\n156\t    /// Unix timestamp (ms) when backoff expires\n157\t    backoff_until_ms: Option<i64>,\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\timpl LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n161\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n162\t        Self {\n163\t            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n164\t        }\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n168\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n169\t    pub fn check(\n170\t        &self,\n171\t        ip: &str,\n172\t        limit: u64,\n173\t        window_ms: u64,\n174\t        failed_threshold: u32,\n175\t        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n176\t        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n177\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n178\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n179\t        let now = now_ms();\n180\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n181\t\n182\t        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n183\t        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n184\t            if backoff_until > now {\n185\t                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n186\t                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n187\t            }\n188\t            // Backoff expired, clear it\n189\t            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n190\t        }\n191\t\n192\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n193\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n194\t\n195\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n196\t        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n197\t            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n198\t            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n199\t            state.failed_count = failed;\n200\t\n201\t            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n202\t                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n203\t                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n204\t                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n205\t                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n206\t            }\n207\t\n208\t            return (false, None);\n209\t        }\n210\t\n211\t        // Record this request\n212\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n213\t        (true, None)\n214\t    }\n215\t\n216\t    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n217\t    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n218\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n219\t        inner.state.remove(ip);\n220\t    }\n221\t\n222\t    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n223\t    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n224\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n225\t        let now = now_ms();\n226\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n227\t\n228\t        state.failed_count += 1;\n229\t\n230\t        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n231\t            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n232\t            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n233\t            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n234\t            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n235\t        }\n236\t\n237\t        None\n238\t    }\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\timpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n242\t    fn default() -> Self {\n243\t        Self::new()\n244\t    }\n245\t}\n246\t\n247\t/// In-memory rate limiter for search UI (local backend only).\n248\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n249\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n250\tpub struct LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n251\t    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner>>,\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n255\tstruct LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner {\n256\t    /// Map of IP -> request_timestamps_ms\n257\t    state: HashMap<String, Vec<i64>>,\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\timpl LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n261\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n262\t        Self {\n263\t            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner::default())),\n264\t        }\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    /// Check rate limit for search UI.\n268\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n269\t    pub fn check(\n270\t        &self,\n271\t        ip: &str,\n272\t        limit: u64,\n273\t        window_ms: u64,\n274\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n275\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n276\t        let now = now_ms();\n277\t        let timestamps = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n278\t\n279\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n280\t        timestamps.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n281\t\n282\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n283\t        if timestamps.len() >= limit as usize {\n284\t            return (false, None);\n285\t        }\n286\t\n287\t        // Record this request\n288\t        timestamps.push(now);\n289\t        (true, None)\n290\t    }\n291\t}\n292\t\n293\timpl Default for LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n294\t    fn default() -> Self {\n295\t        Self::new()\n296\t    }\n297\t}\n298\t\n299\t/// Get current time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.\n300\tfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n301\t    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n302\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n303\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n304\t        .as_millis() as i64\n305\t}\n306\t\n307\t/// Metrics observer for replica selection events.\n308\t///\n309\t/// Reports selection scores and exploration events to Prometheus.\n310\tstruct self.metrics.inc_replica_selection_exploration();\n321\t    }\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t/// Shared application state for admin endpoints.\n325\t#[derive(Clone)]\n326\tpub struct AppState {\n327\t    pub   pub fn with_redis(\n381\t        config: MiroirConfig,\n382\t        metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n383\t        redis_store: Option<RedisTaskStore>,\n384\t        pod_id: String,\n385\t        seal_key: SealKey,\n386\t    ) -> Self {\n387\t        // Build initial topology from config\n388\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(\n389\t            config.shards,\n390\t            config.replica_groups,\n391\t            config.replication_factor as usize,\n392\t        );\n393\t\n394\t        for node_config in &config.nodes {\n395\t            let node = Node::new(\n396\t                NodeId::new(node_config.id.clone()),\n397\t                node_config.address.clone(),\n398\t                node_config.replica_group,\n399\t            );\n400\t            // Start nodes in Joining state - health checker will promote to Active\n401\t            topology.add_node(node);\n402\t        }\n403\t\n404\t        let version_state = VersionState::new(\n405\t            config.node_master_key.clone(),\n406\t            config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect(),\n407\t        );\n408\t\n409\t        // Select task registry backend based on config\n410\t        let task_registry = match config.task_store.backend.as_str() {\n411\t            \"redis\" if redis_store.is_some() => {\n412\t                let store = redis_store.as_ref().unwrap().clone();\n413\t                store.migrate().expect(\"Redis migration failed\");\n414\t                TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(Arc::new(store))\n415\t            }\n416\t            \"sqlite\" if !config.task_store.path.is_empty() => {\n417\t                TaskRegistryImpl::sqlite(&config.task_store.path)\n418\t                    .expect(\"Failed to open SQLite task store\")\n419\t            }\n420\t            _ => TaskRegistryImpl::in_memory(),\n421\t        };\n422\t\n423\t        let topology_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology));\n424\t\n425\t        // Initialize rebalancer and migration coordinator\n426\t        let rebalancer_config = RebalancerConfig {\n427\t            max_concurrent_migrations: config.rebalancer.max_concurrent_migrations,\n428\t            migration_timeout_s: config.rebalancer.migration_timeout_s,\n429\t            auto_rebalance_on_recovery: config.rebalancer.auto_rebalance_on_recovery,\n430\t            migration_batch_size: 1000,\n431\t            migration_batch_delay_ms: 100,\n432\t        };\n433\t\n434\t        let migration_config = MigrationConfig {\n435\t            drain_timeout: std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n436\t            skip_delta_pass: false,\n437\t            anti_entropy_enabled: config.anti_entropy.enabled,\n438\t        };\n439\t\n440\t        let migration_coordinator = Arc::new(RwLock::new(\n441\t            MigrationCoordinator::new(migration_config.clone())\n442\t        ));\n443\t\n444\t        // Create migration executor for actual HTTP document migration\n445\t        use miroir_core::rebalancer::HttpMigrationExecutor;\n446\t        let migration_executor = Arc::new(HttpMigrationExecutor::new(\n447\t            config.node_master_key.clone(),\n448\t            config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n449\t        ));\n450\t\n451\t        let rebalancer = Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n452\t            rebalancer_config.clone(),\n453\t            topology_arc.clone(),\n454\t            migration_config.clone(),\n455\t        ).with_migration_executor(migration_executor));\n456\t\n457\t        // Create rebalancer metrics\n458\t        let rebalancer_metrics = Arc::new(RwLock::new(RebalancerMetrics::default()));\n459\t\n460\t        // Get or create task store for rebalancer worker\n461\t        let task_store: Option<Arc<dyn TaskStore>> = match config.task_store.backend.as_str() {\n462\t            \"redis\" => {\n463\t                redis_store.as_ref().map(|s| Arc::new(s.clone()) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>)\n464\t            }\n465\t            \"sqlite\" if !config.task_store.path.is_empty() => {\n466\t                Some(Arc::new(miroir_core::task_store::SqliteTaskStore::open(\n467\t                    std::path::Path::new(&config.task_store.path)\n468\t                ).expect(\"Failed to open SQLite task store\")) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>)\n469\t            }\n470\t            _ => None,\n471\t        };\n472\t\n473\t        // Create rebalancer worker if task store is available\n474\t        let rebalancer_worker = if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n475\t            let worker_config = RebalancerWorkerConfig {\n476\t                max_concurrent_migrations: config.rebalancer.max_concurrent_migrations,\n477\t                lease_ttl_secs: 10,\n478\t                lease_renewal_interval_ms: 2000,\n479\t                migration_batch_size: 1000,\n480\t                migration_batch_delay_ms: 100,\n481\t                event_channel_capacity: 100,\n482\t            };\n483\t\n484\t            // Create metrics callback for rebalancer operations\n485\t            let metrics_for_worker = metrics.clone();\n486\t            let rebalancer_metrics_callback: RebalancerMetricsCallback = Arc::new(\n487\t                move |in_progress: bool, docs_migrated: Option<u64>, duration_secs: Option<f64>| {\n488\t                    if in_progress {\n489\t                        metrics_for_worker.set_rebalance_in_progress(true);\n490\t                    } else {\n491\t                        metrics_for_worker.set_rebalance_in_progress(false);\n492\t                    }\n493\t                    if let Some(count) = docs_migrated {\n494\t                        metrics_for_worker.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(count);\n495\t                    }\n496\t                    if let Some(duration) = duration_secs {\n497\t                        metrics_for_worker.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n498\t                    }\n499\t                }\n500\t            );\n501\t\n502\t            Some(Arc::new(RebalancerWorker::with_metrics(\n503\t                worker_config,\n504\t                topology_arc.clone(),\n505\t                store.clone(),\n506\t                rebalancer.clone(),\n507\t                migration_coordinator.clone(),\n508\t                rebalancer_metrics.clone(),\n509\t                pod_id.clone(),\n510\t                Some(rebalancer_metrics_callback),\n511\t            )))\n512\t        } else {\n513\t            None\n514\t        };\n515\t\n516\t        // Create settings Arc::new(miroir_core::settings::SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(store.clone()))\n519\t        } else {\n520\t            Arc::new(miroir_core::settings::SettingsBroadcast::new())\n521\t        };\n522\t\n523\t        // Check if task store is available before moving it\n524\t        let has_task_store = task_store.is_some();\n525\t\n526\t        // Create drift reconciler worker (§13.5) if task store is available\n527\t        let drift_reconciler = if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n528\t            let node_addresses = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n529\t            let drift_config = miroir_core::rebalancer_worker::DriftReconcilerConfig {\n530\t                interval_s: config.settings_drift_check.interval_s,\n531\t                auto_repair: config.settings_drift_check.auto_repair,\n532\t                lease_ttl_secs: 10,\n533\t                lease_renewal_interval_ms: 2000,\n534\t            };\n535\t            Some(Arc::new(miroir_core::rebalancer_worker::DriftReconciler::new(\n536\t                drift_config,\n537\t                settings_broadcast.clone(),\n538\t                store.clone(),\n539\t                node_addresses,\n540\t                config.node_master_key.clone(),\n541\t                pod_id.clone(),\n542\t            )))\n543\t        } else {\n544\t            None\n545\t        };\n546\t\n547\t        // Create anti-entropy worker (plan §13.8) if task store is available\n548\t        let anti_entropy_worker = if config.anti_entropy.enabled {\n549\t            if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n550\t                let ae_worker_config = miroir_core::rebalancer_worker::AntiEntropyWorkerConfig::from_schedule(\n551\t                    &config.anti_entropy.schedule\n552\t                );\n553\t                let metrics_for_ae_1 = metrics.clone();\n554\t                let metrics_for_ae_2 = metrics.clone();\n555\t                let metrics_for_ae_3 = metrics.clone();\n556\t                let metrics_for_ae_4 = metrics.clone();\n557\t                let mut ae_worker = miroir_core::rebalancer_worker::AntiEntropyWorker::new(\n558\t                    ae_worker_config,\n559\t check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n770\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n771\t        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n772\t\n773\t        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n774\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n775\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n776\t            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n777\t            if healthy.len() < required {\n778\t                return false;\n779\t            }\n780\t        }\n781\t\n782\t        true\n783\t    }\n784\t\n785\t    /// self.metrics.set_rebalance_in_progress(in_progress);\n791\t\n792\t            // Calculate delta for documents migrated counter\n793\t            let current_total = rebalancer_metrics.documents_migrated_total;\n794\t            let previous = self.previous_docs_migrated.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n795\t            if current_total > previous {\n796\t                let delta = current_total - previous;\n797\t                self.metrics.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(delta);\n798\t                self.previous_docs_migrated.store(current_total, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n799\t            }\n800\t\n801\t            let duration = rebalancer_metrics.current_duration_secs();\n802\t            if duration > 0.0 {\n803\t                self.metrics.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n804\t            }\n805\t        }\n806\t    }\n807\t}\n808\t\n809\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/topology (plan §10 JSON shape).\n810\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n811\tpub struct TopologyResponse {\n812\t    pub shards: u32,\n813\t    pub replication_factor: StatusCode>\n840\twhere\n841\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n842\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n843\t{\n844\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n845\t    let topo = {\n855\t        false\n856\t    };\n857\t\n858\t    // Build node info list\n859\t    let nodes: Vec<NodeInfo> = topo\n860\t        .nodes()\n861\t        .map(|n| NodeInfo {\n862\t            id:      replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n877\t        nodes,\n878\t        degraded_node_count: degraded_count,\n879\t        rebalance_in_progress,\n880\t        fully_covered,\n881\t    };\n882\t\n883\t    Ok(Json(response))\n884\t}\n885\t\n886\t/// GET /_miroir/shards — shard → node mapping table.\n887\tpub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\n888\twhere\n889\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n890\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n891\t{\n892\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n893\t    let      let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n899\t\n900\t        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n901\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n902\t            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n903\t            for node_id in assigned {\n904\t                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n905\t readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\n915\tpub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\n916\twhere\n917\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n918\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n919\t{\n920\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n921\t    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n922\t\n923\t    if ready {\n924\t {\n929\t            // Auto-mark ready on first successful quorum check\n930\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n931\t            Ok(\"\")\n932\t        } else {\n933\t            Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n934\t        }\n935\t    }\n936\t}\n937\t\n938\t/// GET /_miroir/metrics — admin-key-gated Prometheus metrics.\n939\tpub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Response\n940\twhere\n941\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n942\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n943\t{\n944\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n945\t    match
